summary: Adversarial critique of kernel-spec-v0 against decided law: floors, envelope, D2 invariants, checker prototype scope

findings.problem: FN-4 lets a 'law-test harness' discharge laws into optimizer-usable facts. A property-test harness is neither deterministic-checker-grade proof, a runtime check under trap=abort, nor a verified proof artifact — the only admissible discharge forms (round3-synthesis-safety-envelope.md, Decided now: "'proof' is defined as deterministic-checker or verified-proof-artifact discharge"; Layer-2 discharge preference). A tested-but-unproven law consumed by the optimizer is exactly the third state the verification spine bans (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md line 33: law annotations must be checked obligations, not trusted assertions; round-3 spine amendment: toolchain-gated ledger entries are the SOLE trusted-assertion class).
findings.fix: Restrict law discharge to (a) the stated-and-checked channel — static proof, runtime check under trap=abort, or verified proof artifact (round-3 Layer-0 channel (a)) — or (b) gated-family ledger entries. Testing may inform gate review of a ledger entry but never directly licenses optimizer use of a law.

findings.problem: "Accepted programs have no undefined behavior" states D1 unconditionally. The owner ruled D1a as a Rust-class CONDITIONAL envelope (decision-gates.md, Owner rulings D1a/D0a), and round-3 recorded the honest Layer-4 statement as position-invariant law: safety holds CONDITIONAL on ABI-well-behaved foreign code and the declared TCB (round3-synthesis-safety-envelope.md, Layer 4 and decision-log entry (c)). Even a pure-kernel program sits on the undelimited TCB (allocator, runtime, compiler/checker).
findings.fix: Restate SCOPE-3 conditionally: accepted programs have no UB conditional on the declared TCB (and, once gated/FFI constructs are linked, on ABI-well-behaved foreign code), cross-referencing the Layer-4 envelope statement.

findings.problem: Defaulting non-borrowed `let` bindings to `stack` "unless the initializer is an explicit allocation call" elides a contract-bearing fact. The round-2 MEMORY floor requires EVERY allocation to carry a writer-stated storage/lifetime contract (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md, MEMORY line; decision-gates.md round-2 floors: "explicit storage contracts"), the round-2 cut list bans "elision of contract-bearing facts", and the D2 corrected ruling decides uniform verbose-everywhere annotation with no elision (decision-gates.md, D2 corrected ruling). The "unless" clause also violates the spec's own META-3 (no exception clauses).
findings.fix: Make the storage class explicit on every binding — e.g. fold storage into the mandatory mode (`let x: own stack i32 = ...;`) or require alloc_stack/alloc_arena/alloc_heap calls uniformly — and delete the default clause.

findings.problem: SCOPE-1 plus the LAYER exclusion define the priced kernel as writer-facing-only, excluding the obligation ledger, FFI declaration frames, and capability predicates from the budget (3208 tokens, decision-gates.md Calibration audit v0). Round-4 decided law says "kernel (D1a ownership calculus + ledger + FFI frames + capabilities) is priced FIRST" (decision-gates.md, Round-4 teachability states), and round-2 promoted C004 capability predicates to "a required core kernel component" (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md, Round-1 verdicts affected, concurrency-model). The spec both narrows the decided kernel-pricing unit and silently drops a required component; the calibration audit priced the wrong artifact.
findings.fix: Add capability-predicate rules and the writer-visible schemas of the ledger and FFI frames (normative stubs at minimum) to this document's counted kernel mass and re-run the calibration audit; any narrowing of the round-4 kernel definition requires an owner ruling with a declared spec delta per META-5.

findings.problem: "There is no third state: nothing writer-stated is trusted unchecked" restates the PRE-amendment spine. The adopted amendment (round3-synthesis-safety-envelope.md, Round-1/2 amendments, first bullet; decision-gates.md round-3: "no WRITER-EMITTABLE third state; toolchain-gated ledger entries are the sole trusted-assertion class") is the legislated form, and FN-4's own reference to gated-channel discharge contradicts SCOPE-2 as written — a kernel program relying on a gated law lives in a state SCOPE-2 says does not exist.
findings.fix: Reword to "no writer-emittable third state", name toolchain-gated ledger entries as the sole trusted-assertion class (specified separately), and note that the strict reading governs until the amendment is formally issued (round-3 Decided now, last item).

findings.problem: bool has two identities and two spellings: TYPE-1 lists `bool` as a primitive, GRAM-5 says it is prelude `enum Bool { True(); False(); }`, and FORM-5 defines literals `true`/`false` alongside variant spellings `True()`/`False()`. This violates FORM-1/META-1 and the round-2 canonical-form floor (one spelling per semantic construct — round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md SURFACE line; decision-gates.md round-2 floors). A writer cannot know whether match arms on a bool are legal or which constructor to emit.
findings.fix: Pick one representation: prelude Bool enum (remove `bool` from TYPE-1 and `true`/`false` from FORM-5) or a primitive bool with a defined dispatch form. One spelling, stated once.

findings.problem: FN-3 requires conformance to be "declared explicitly per (type, contract)", but `item := fn_decl | struct_decl | enum_decl | contract_decl` has no conformance-declaration production, and `fn_sig`/`law` in contract_decl are undefined nonterminals. Conformance is unwritable, so contract-bounded generics (FN-2/FN-3) — part of the decided contract-group kernel (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md, CONTRACT KERNEL line) — can never be exercised. Direct writer/checker divergence.
findings.fix: Add a `conform_decl` production to `item` (naming type, contract, and member bindings) plus `fn_sig` and `law` productions.

findings.problem: The place grammar has no dereference/borrow-projection form (`place := IDENT | place.IDENT | index(...)`), so reading or writing "through that borrow" — the only legal access under a live `&mut` per OWN-5 — is inexpressible. The D1a calculus (decision-gates.md, Owner rulings D1a) is unusable as specified: borrows can be created but never used.
findings.fix: Add a deref place node (e.g. `place := ... | "deref" "(" place ")"`) with its region/exclusivity typing rules in section 5.

findings.problem: Referenced nonterminals are never defined: `type`, `mode`, `generics`, `targs`, `effects`, `region_params`, `path`, `literal`, `type_list`, `param_list`, `arg_list`, `binder_list`, `LABEL`, `STRING`; struct/enum construction expressions are missing entirely. GRAM-1 claims LL(1) and 1:1 core-tree isomorphism over a grammar that is not closed. Under the round-4 no-shadow-spec rule (decision-gates.md: "writer-facing schemas count as spec mass") the undefined productions are uncounted spec mass distorting the calibration audit baseline.
findings.fix: Complete the grammar (all productions, all token classes, construction forms), then re-price the spec and update the audit note in decision-gates.md.

findings.problem: The storage-contract vocabulary omits the decided `foreign-shared` allocation-site field: round-3 decided now that foreign-retainable memory is pre-declared foreign-shared at its allocation site as "one field on the existing writer-stated storage-contract floor", and that compiler-inferred call-site demotion is REJECTED (round3-synthesis-safety-envelope.md, Decided now; round-2 MEMORY floor amendment (b)). The spec is silent, so the storage grammar must break when FFI frames land, and the demotion rejection is recorded nowhere normative.
findings.fix: Reserve the foreign-shared field in STOR-1's contract vocabulary now, with a rule stating it is only legal in programs containing gated FFI frames, and record that call-site demotion is a floor violation.

findings.problem: The decided machine-readable report family is missing: per-function check reports (round3-synthesis-safety-envelope.md, Decided now: "Machine-readable per-function check reports join the report family now"), per-function lifetime reports (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md, MEMORY floor), and check-density/elision reports as the lint-class containment channel (round-3 Layer 1). DIAG-2 surfaces ops in the artifact but omits the report family that decided law attaches to it.
findings.fix: Add a DIAG rule enumerating the normative report family (check reports, lifetime reports, check-density/elision reports) as toolchain output accompanying the canonical artifact.

findings.problem: Acceptance artifact-checkability has no rule: round-3 cross-cutting invariant (4) and decision-log entry (d) require proof objects and check instrumentation to travel with the program so that 'accepted' is artifact-checkable, not process-relative (round3-synthesis-safety-envelope.md). The spec defines acceptance (SCOPE-2) purely as a process outcome.
findings.fix: Add a rule: the canonical elaborated artifact embeds proof objects and check instrumentation, and acceptance is decidable from the artifact alone across toolchain versions.

findings.problem: The contract-edit gating floor is unstated: round-2 decided that editing a declared contract (signatures, law bundles, lifetime contracts) is a privileged, gated toolchain operation so a writer cannot launder a hallucinated body by rewriting the declaration (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md, Additions required because the writer is an AI), extended by round-3 into the unified gating regime (round3-synthesis-safety-envelope.md, gating regime unification). This is writer-facing law — it bounds what the writer may edit — and the kernel spec omits it entirely.
findings.fix: Add a rule: contract, signature, law, and storage-contract edits are one privileged gated toolchain operation with one audit trail, outside steady-state writer capability.

findings.problem: "The compiler may eliminate a check only by proof" leaves 'proof' undefined. Round-3 legislated that proof means deterministic-checker-grade or verified-proof-artifact discharge, and fenced the solver: it may only PROMOTE performance facts, never gate acceptance, and never elide safety-relevant checks until a proof-checker TCB exists (round3-synthesis-safety-envelope.md, Decided now; decision-gates.md round-3 summary). As written, raw SMT output could count as proof — a permissive reading that contradicts the solver fence.
findings.fix: Define 'proof' per the round-3 legislated definition in OP-4 (or a shared definition rule) and state the solver fence: solver-promoted facts are performance-ledger only and never license check elision.

findings.problem: `writes('static)` references a `'static` region defined nowhere: OWN-3 admits only lexical regions and caller-supplied region parameters, FORM-3's region identifiers have no tick form, and the spec has no global/static declarations for the effect to range over. A writer cannot produce a conforming main, and the checker has no rule to check it against.
findings.fix: Either define the static region and global state as kernel constructs, or reduce main's permitted row to `allocates(heap), traps` and delete the reference.

findings.problem: The decided error-handling replacement is only half-present. Missing: verifier-checked Result propagation with auto-derived context/cause chains at zero hand-written tokens per site — explicitly the argument that neutralized unwinding — and the language-enforced Result-vs-trap classification rule plus anti-sentinel lints (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md, ERRORS line and Replacing exceptions). Bare manual match propagation reopens the objection the round-2 verdict priced away.
findings.fix: Add the propagation operation with auto-derived context chains and the Result-vs-trap classification rule, or record an explicit deferral with its spec delta per META-5.

findings.problem: The prelude (Bool, Result, Option, the iadd/isub/imul/idiv/ineg/comparison families, cvt, index, alloc_arena, alloc_heap, band/bor/bnot) is normative writer-facing surface referenced throughout but specified nowhere and not counted in the 3208-token pricing — a shadow spec violating the round-4 no-shadow-spec rule (decision-gates.md, Round-4: "writer-facing schemas count as spec mass").
findings.fix: Publish the full prelude signature listing (with modes, effect rows, and generic bounds) as a counted normative annex and re-run the calibration audit.

findings.problem: The closures/dispatch replacement floors are named but not specified: no guarantee that env-struct dispatch monomorphizes to direct calls (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md, Replacing closures: "GUARANTEED language capability — never fn-pointer+env indirection"), none of the mandated exactness diagnostics (unused env field, used-but-uninitialized field, dead dispatch variant, non-exhaustive tag dispatch, signature mismatch — each naming struct, function, construction site), and typed operation tables with construction-checked payload/table coupling and the one-canonical-table-per-(variant-set x operation) one-definition rule (Replacing dyn dispatch) are absent entirely.
findings.fix: Add rules for the direct-call guarantee and the mandated diagnostic set; add the typed-operation-table construct with its one-definition and construction-coupling rules, or record explicit deferrals with spec deltas.

findings.problem: `array<T, N>` introduces const generics with no constant-expression rules. The round-2 floor is a "closed, decidable, trivially-terminating constant-expression sublanguage" (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md, COMPILE-TIME COMPUTATION; decision-gates.md round-2 floors: "closed constant-expression sublanguage only"). With N's expression language unspecified, writer and checker will diverge on what lengths are writable and when two array types are equal.
findings.fix: Add CONST rules defining the closed sublanguage (built-in arithmetic/layout/target-parameter constants), or restrict N to literal integer constants in v0 and say so explicitly.

findings.problem: "Strict IEEE 754 by default" is incoherent under one-mode-per-node (there are no defaults if every node names `.strict`), and remanding fast-math/approximation permissions to a LAYER decides the placement of a question numeric-semantics still holds open (decision-gates.md round-1: numeric-semantics no_decision; round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md amendment: default-mode policy question remains open; round-3 narrowed the open residue but decided only overflow, not float relaxation placement).
findings.fix: Reword: "v0 defines only `.strict` float modes; each node names its mode." Record approximation/fast-math modes as an open numeric-semantics question rather than a decided LAYER.

findings.problem: Region spelling is internally inconsistent: sections 5-6 use tick form (`'a`, `arena<'a>`, `&mut 'a p`) while FORM-3 gives regions plain `[a-z][a-z0-9_]*` identifiers and GRAM-4 uses tickless REGIONID. Two spellings for one construct violates FORM-1/META-1 and the canonical-form floor (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md, SURFACE).
findings.fix: Pick one region spelling and use it in every rule, grammar production, and example.

findings.problem: "Every rule is locally decidable at its node given declared types in scope" overstates the round-4 invariant (decision-gates.md: "zero context-dependent rules" — a regularity constraint on spellings/rules, not node-local decidability) and is falsified by the spec's own flow-dependent checks: OWN-5 exclusivity and OWN-6 liveness require knowledge of other live borrows, not just the node and declared types.
findings.fix: Reword META-2 to the round-4 formulation (no context-dependent spellings or rule variants) and drop the node-local decidability claim.

findings.problem: The effect-row vocabulary is instantiated while its named blocking evidence is undischarged: region/effect verifier exemplar cards (Cyclone, MLKit, Koka, DPJ) are "blocking for the effect-row floor" (round3-synthesis-safety-envelope.md, Experiments needed; round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md, aliasing-model amendment: currently ZERO cards). The spec draft does not record this gate on section 9.
findings.fix: Mark section 9 in the status header as gated on the region/effect exemplar carding before ratification.

findings.problem: Match arm binder modes are unspecified: whether `Variant(x)` moves the payload into x, and what binders denote when the scrutinee is a shared or exclusive borrow, is undefined — a direct writer/checker divergence point inside the D1a calculus the checker prototype has necessarily already decided one way (decision-gates.md, Checker-core prototype).
findings.fix: Add binder-mode rules to sections 3/5 (e.g. binders inherit the scrutinee's mode; an owned scrutinee is consumed by the match).

findings.problem: The optional stated-and-checked channel (loop invariants, ranges, loop-bounded disjointness, law obligations — fact-creating but always checked; round3-synthesis-safety-envelope.md, Layer 0 channel (a)) has no kernel surface, and the spec does not say whether a passed `check` is fact-creating (its fact joining the proven set on the dominated path, the A005 guard-then-assume pattern) or purely a trap assertion.
findings.fix: State check's fact-creating status in OP-5 and record the fuller stated-and-checked vocabulary as an explicitly deferred channel with its spec delta.

findings.problem: Section 5 presents an invented rule set without recording the ratification precondition the owner attached: "formalized-calculus adoption (Featherweight Rust/Austral-class) required before ratification" and the adopted checker-simplification levers (decision-gates.md, Owner rulings D1a/D0a and Checker-core prototype). A reader of the spec alone cannot see that OWN-1..9 are provisional pending calculus alignment.
findings.fix: Add to the status header (or section 5 preamble) that OWN rules must be reconciled against a formalized minimal calculus before ratification, citing the D1a gate.

missingRules: CAP-1..n: type-level capability predicates (Send/Sync-class per C004, amended scope: data races prevented, general race conditions not) — required core kernel component (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md CONTRACT KERNEL + concurrency amendment; round-3 Layer 0; part of the round-4 kernel pricing unit in decision-gates.md).

STOR-5: the `foreign-shared` allocation-site field on the storage contract, plus the recorded rejection of compiler-inferred call-site demotion (round3-synthesis-safety-envelope.md, Decided now; round-2 MEMORY amendment (b)).

GATE-1: contract-edit gating / unified gating regime — contract, signature, law-bundle, and storage-contract edits are one privileged gated toolchain operation with one audit trail, outside steady-state writer capability (round-2 Additions; round-3 gating unification).

DIAG-3: machine-readable report family — per-function check reports, per-function lifetime reports, and check-density/elision reports accompanying the canonical artifact (round-2 MEMORY floor; round-3 Decided now).

DIAG-4: acceptance artifact-checkability — proof objects and check instrumentation travel with the canonical artifact; acceptance is decidable from the artifact alone (round-3 cross-cutting invariant 4, decision-log (d)).
DIAG-5: trap-report content — machine-readable, with descriptive CFI/frame-pointer stack attribution and no executable unwind semantics (round-2 ERRORS floor and AI-writer additions).
ERR-3: language-enforced Result-vs-trap classification rule; verifier-checked Result propagation with auto-derived context/cause chains; anti-sentinel lint contract (round-2 Replacing exceptions).

DISP-1: typed operation tables — named fields, per-row signature checks, construction-checked payload/table coupling, one canonical table per (variant-set x operation) with ad-hoc switch sites lowered or flagged (round-2 Replacing dyn dispatch).

FN-7: env-struct guarantees and mandated exactness diagnostics — guaranteed direct-call monomorphization; unused env field, used-but-uninitialized field, dead dispatch variant, non-exhaustive tag dispatch, signature mismatch, each naming struct/function/construction site (round-2 Replacing closures).
CONST-1: the closed, decidable, trivially-terminating constant-expression sublanguage governing const generics and array lengths (round-2 COMPILE-TIME COMPUTATION floor).

GRAM: conformance-declaration item production, fn_sig/law productions, full type/mode/effects/generics/targs/path/literal/token-class productions, and struct/enum construction expressions (closes GRAM-1's isomorphism claim).
GRAM/OWN: dereference place form with region/exclusivity rules, and match-binder mode rules (without these the D1a borrow calculus is unusable as written).
PRELUDE-1: normative, token-counted prelude signature annex (Bool/Result/Option, arithmetic/comparison/boolean families, cvt, index, alloc_* ops) per the round-4 no-shadow-spec rule.
PROG/OWN: definition of the 'static region and global state, or removal of `writes('static)` from PROG-2.

SCOPE/LEDGER: a writer-visible normative stub for the gated fact-boundary family — one family, one per-fact obligation ledger, manifest-free members unrepresentable, writer cannot author or edit — so kernel programs' relationship to gated law (e.g. FN-4 discharge) is defined and the round-4 pricing unit is complete (round-3 Layer 2; decision-gates.md round-4).

findings.problem: The escape condition is INVERTED and unsound: 'may not be stored into a value or returned unless the destination's declared region outlives-or-equals 'a' permits exactly the dangling case — a borrow in inner region 'a stored into a destination in an enclosing (longer-lived) region passes the test and outlives its referent (use-after-free representable, contradicting SCOPE-3 and directive D1 in notes/user-directives.md). The reference checker implements the OPPOSITE (sound) direction: prototype/checker/checker.py:170-177 rejects when the borrow's region does not outlive the destination's, and test_checker.py:80-100 (test_escape_inner_region_to_outer) rejects a program the spec text as written accepts. Spec text and checker already diverge.
findings.fix: Rewrite: 'it may not be stored into a value or returned unless 'a outlives-or-equals the destination's declared region.' Add a spec-CI test tying OWN-4's text to the checker's OWN-4 test vectors.

findings.problem: 'let bindings of non-borrowed values are stack unless the initializer is an explicit allocation call' is (a) a defaulting rule — banned by the round-4 zero-context-dependence invariant ('no defaulting', round4-synthesis-teachability.md line 32) and by META-2; (b) an 'unless' exception clause — banned by META-3/empty-exception-lists; (c) a direct contradiction of STOR-1 ('Every allocation names its storage class explicitly') since plain-let stack allocations name nothing; and (d) a violation of the round-2 decided floor 'every allocation carries a writer-stated, compiler-checked storage/lifetime contract' (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md line 29), which round4-synthesis line 8 explicitly calls majority-path mandatory law.
findings.fix: Make storage explicit in the one uniform spelling: either every let names its storage class in the mode position (e.g. 'let x: own stack T = ...') or every initializer is an explicit allocation call including alloc_stack<T>(v). Delete the default and the unless-clause.

findings.problem: bool has two contradictory definitions and two spellings: TYPE-1 lists bool as a PRIMITIVE type while GRAM-5 says 'bool is enum Bool { True(); False(); } in the prelude'. FORM-5 additionally blesses literals 'true'/'false', giving the same construct a literal spelling in expression position and a variant spelling (True()/False()) in match-arm position — a one-spelling-per-construct violation with positionally-resolved spelling (round4-synthesis lines 31-32) and a duplicated, mutually inconsistent normative fact (META-1/META-4 fail on the spec's own terms).
findings.fix: Pick one story and state it once: remove bool from TYPE-1's primitive list, remove true/false from FORM-5, define Bool/True()/False() solely as the prelude enum with the variant spelling in both expression and pattern position (matches GRAM-5's no-if design). Fix the enum name/type-name case mismatch (bool vs Bool) at the same time.

findings.problem: 'each law is either checked by the toolchain's law-test harness or discharged by the gated channel' creates a third trust state: a property-test harness samples inputs — it neither proves the law nor attaches a runtime check at the optimizer's point of use, yet FN-4 makes harness-passed laws 'optimizer-usable facts'. This contradicts SCOPE-2's own 'There is no third state' and the round-3 spine amendment ('no WRITER-EMITTABLE third state; toolchain-gated ledger entries are the sole trusted-assertion class' — round3-synthesis-safety-envelope.md line 82, decision-gates.md line 62). A false-but-test-passing associativity law consumed by a reassociation pass is a silent miscompile.
findings.fix: Restrict optimizer-usable laws to gated-family ledger discharge or verified proof artifacts (cross-reference the gated channel only). The law-test harness may remain as non-normative pre-gate evidence but must not be a trust source; reword FN-4 accordingly.

findings.problem: No-shadow-spec violations: the spec normatively references writer-facing artifacts whose schemas live nowhere in the artifact — the machine-readable trap report (SCOPE-3, OP-5, EFF-3), the diagnostics vocabulary and node-path format (DIAG-1), the site-enumerated edit list (ERR-2), the elaborated re-read artifact format (DIAG-2, STOR-3), and the prelude contents (GRAM-5, ERR-1, PROG-1, STOR-2). Round-4 decided law adopts no-shadow-spec with an extended perimeter naming exactly these items as counted normative mass (round4-synthesis-teachability.md lines 11, 21, 39; decision-gates.md line 84). notes/calibration-audit-v0.json confirms the priced 3208 tokens exclude them, so the calibration baseline the spec exists to establish (its stated purpose (a)) undercounts decided-law spec mass.
findings.fix: Add sections for the trap-report schema, diagnostics/witness vocabulary, edit-list schema, elaborated-artifact format, and the prelude inventory to this artifact (they may be appendices but must be inside the counted document), then re-run the calibration audit and restate the baseline in decision-gates.md.

findings.problem: Region names have two spellings inside the spec: OWN-2 writes '&r T' (bare identifier), while OWN-3/OWN-4/STOR-1/STOR-2/EFF-1/PROG-2 write ''a'/''static' (apostrophe form), and GRAM-4's REGIONID terminal is never lexically defined. FORM-3 assigns regions the [a-z][a-z0-9_]* class, which excludes the apostrophe form used by six other rules — the artifact itself exhibits two spellings for one construct (META-1 / round-4 one-spelling invariant) and leaves the canonical one undecidable.
findings.fix: Define REGIONID's lexical form once in FORM-3 (pick one: bare lowercase ident or apostrophe form), and rewrite every region occurrence in OWN/STOR/EFF/PROG to that single spelling.

findings.problem: The 'canonical grammar' (load-bearing per SCOPE-2 acceptance) is not closed: nonterminals/terminals mode, type, generics, region_params, effects, targs, path, literal, LABEL, STRING, fn_sig, law, and all list separators (param_list, arg_list, type_list, binder_list) are used but never defined. There is also no spelling for supplying region arguments at call sites even though functions take region_params and FN-2/round-2 law forbids inference — a writer cannot spell a call to a region-polymorphic function at all, and every implementer will invent divergent syntax.
findings.fix: Add productions for every undefined nonterminal, define LABEL/STRING/REGIONID lexically, specify list separators and trailing rules, and add explicit region arguments to the call production (e.g. call := path targs? rargs? '(' arg_list? ')').

findings.problem: The LL(1) claim is false for the stated productions: 'place := IDENT | place "." IDENT | ...' is left-recursive (never LL(1)); expr's alternatives place and call share FIRST token IDENT (FIRST/FIRST conflict); and 'index targs (place, expr)' is derivable both as a place and as a call (path='index'), so the grammar is ambiguous. A normative property the grammar itself violates guarantees implementer divergence in how conflicts are resolved.
findings.fix: Refactor to LL(1) form (place := IDENT place_suffix*; distinguish call by lookahead-free means such as a distinct production ordering the spec defines) and add a spec-CI check that mechanically verifies the LL(1)/unambiguity claims over the stated productions.

findings.problem: index has two grammar homes: OP-4 presents index<T>(s, i) as a call-shaped operation (and OP-1 claims 'Every computation is a call-shaped operation'), while GRAM-4 gives index its own special place production. One construct, two syntactic categories — a one-spelling violation and a break of GRAM-1's 1:1 production-to-node isomorphism; a writer cannot tell whether index in expression position is legal, and set/borrow of an indexed element depends on which reading the checker picked.
findings.fix: Choose one home and state it once: either index is only a place form (delete the call reading; OP-4 then governs the place form's check semantics) or only a call returning a borrow (delete it from the place grammar). Cross-reference from the other section by rule ID.

findings.problem: Affine ownership with no copy class: as written, every value — including i32 — is moved at most once, so the second use of any primitive binding is an error, making arithmetic on a variable used twice unwritable. The intended exception exists only as a comment in toolchain code ('read (copy for prims)', prototype/checker/checker.py line 19) — a hidden exception clause (META-3) living outside the artifact (no-shadow-spec violation) and a guaranteed writer/checker divergence.
findings.fix: Add one uniform rule stating which types copy on use (e.g. all TYPE-1 primitives copy; all composites and borrows are affine) and how use-vs-move is spelled or locally determined; align the checker's behavior with the stated rule.

findings.problem: The outlives definition is self-contradicting and partial: 'outlives iff 'a's block strictly encloses 'b's block' is immediately falsified by the next clause (caller-supplied regions have no local block yet outlive everything) — an exception-shaped addendum to an 'iff'. Worse, the relation between two DISTINCT caller-supplied regions is undefined, but OWN-4 needs it to check stores/returns. The prototype checker invents declaration-order semantics (regions.index comparison, prototype/checker/checker.py lines 65-71: earlier-declared caller regions outlive later ones) — normative semantics stated nowhere.
findings.fix: Replace with one total definition: outlives is a partial order = strict block enclosure on local regions; every caller-supplied region outlives every local region; distinct caller-supplied regions are incomparable, and any check requiring an order between them fails (reject, per OWN-8's fail-closed stance). Fix the checker to match.

findings.problem: Conformance 'is declared explicitly per (type, contract)' but the item grammar (GRAM-2: fn_decl | struct_decl | enum_decl | contract_decl) contains no conformance declaration production, and the bound spelling 'fn sum<T: Monoid>' is unparseable because generics has no production. A construct with zero legal spellings contradicts one-spelling-per-construct from the other side and makes the whole contract system unwritable.
findings.fix: Add a conformance item production (e.g. conform_decl := 'conform' TYPEID ':' TYPEID '{' fn_decl* '}') and a generics production covering bounds, and state the at-most-one-conformance coherence rule against it.

findings.problem: The operation inventory is open-ended and exception-laden: 'likewise isub, imul, idiv' plus the parenthetical idiv carve-out ('.trap and .checked only ... no wrap mode') is an inline exception list (META-3 violation); whether ineg has all three modes is ambiguous; OP-3's 'fadd.strict<T> etc.' leaves the float set undefined; irem, float comparisons, and the cvt rows (referenced by TYPE-4) are absent. Round-2 decided law mandates typed operation tables as core machinery (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md line 27; round4-synthesis line 50 counts them as mandatory floors), yet no table exists — every implementer and writer will reconstruct a different inventory.
findings.fix: Replace OP-2/OP-3 prose with one canonical operation table (rows: operation, type domain, available modes, signature, effect row). Mode availability becomes table data, not rule exceptions; the idiv row simply lists two modes. State the table once and cross-reference it.

findings.problem: cvt<i32, i64>(x) is normatively required for every representation change, with '(§7)' claiming its definition, but §7 contains no cvt operation. Which (source, target) pairs exist, which are value-preserving, and which return Result — the entire conversion matrix — is normative content the spec references but does not contain (dangling cross-reference; shadow spec).
findings.fix: Add cvt rows to the §7 canonical operation table with the value-preserving vs Result<T, E> classification stated per pair (or per one uniform width/sign rule), and name the error type E.

findings.problem: Effect rows have no grammar and no canonical ordering — GRAM-3's 'effects' nonterminal is undefined, so 'reads('r) writes('s)' vs 'writes('s) reads('r)' are both plausible spellings, violating FORM-1's one-legal-byte-formatting. Additionally allocates(stack) is incoherent: every function uses the stack, so either stack allocation is silently exempt from the row (a hidden exception, META-3 — and PROG-2's example row for main omits it, implying exactly that) or every function must declare it (contradicting PROG-2). The row's exactness rule (both-ways checking) makes this undecidable for a writer.
findings.fix: Add the effects production with a fixed canonical order and separator, and one rule stating exactly which operations count as allocates (only alloc_arena/alloc_heap calls; frame storage never counts). State the stack exemption as part of the definition of allocates, not as an exception.

findings.problem: writes('static) references a region 'static that no rule defines, and the grammar has no static/global declaration form (items are only fn/struct/enum/contract), so there is nothing writes('static) could describe. 'not exceeding' also introduces an effect-row subsumption relation defined nowhere, in tension with EFF-1's both-ways exactness.
findings.fix: Either define 'static (its allocations, access rules, and a global declaration form) or shrink main's bound to 'allocates(heap), traps'. Define row subsumption in EFF-1 in one sentence ('row A does not exceed B iff every effect in A appears in B') or restate PROG-2 as an exact-row constraint.

findings.problem: There is no dereference spelling: the place grammar (IDENT, field access, index) cannot express reading or writing THROUGH a borrow, and no auto-deref rule is stated (auto-deref would itself be banned context-dependence). OWN-9's 'shared borrows are read-only' therefore has no operational meaning in the surface language. The prototype checker invents holder-based through-borrow semantics (require_readable/require_writable holder logic, prototype/checker/checker.py lines 203-222) that appear in no spec rule — normative behavior living only in toolchain code.
findings.fix: Add an explicit deref place production (e.g. place := ... | 'deref' '(' place ')') plus one OWN rule for access-through-borrow (read via shared or exclusive borrow; write only via exclusive borrow), and align the checker.

findings.problem: Match's ownership semantics are unspecified: nothing states whether the scrutinee is moved or borrowed, or what mode arm binders receive (moved payload fields? borrows in which region?). The OWN section never mentions match, yet Result/Option dispatch (ERR-1) and Bool dispatch (GRAM-5) make match the most common construct in every program — writer and checker will diverge on whether a matched value is dead afterwards.
findings.fix: Add one uniform rule: matching an owned scrutinee moves it and binds payload fields by move; matching through a borrow binds fields as borrows in the borrow's region (or require binder modes to be written explicitly, consistent with OWN-2's no-default stance).

findings.problem: No expression form constructs a struct or an enum-variant value: expr := literal | place | call | match_expr | borrow_expr contains no struct literal, and whether variant construction (e.g. Some<T>(x)) counts as a 'call' is unstated (call := path ... with path undefined). Programs cannot create any composite value under the stated grammar.
findings.fix: State once that constructor application is call-shaped with the type/variant name as path (one production covering struct and variant construction with explicit type arguments), or add dedicated construction productions; define path to include TYPEID segments.

findings.problem: 'every checkable-but-unproven D1-critical fact carries a runtime check' uses the classification 'D1-critical' which the artifact never defines. The class assignment (bounds and checkable alias-disjointness are D1-critical; overflow is a policy class with legal wrap fallback) lives only in round3-synthesis-safety-envelope.md lines 8 and 21 — normative content referenced but not contained; a writer cannot determine from this spec which facts obligate a check.
findings.fix: Add one rule enumerating the D1-critical checkable fact classes in-kernel (array bounds per OP-4; alias-disjointness where a check form exists) and stating that overflow is not in the class; cross-reference it from SCOPE-2 and OP-4 by ID.

findings.problem: The claimed 'exactly one legal byte-level formatting' (FORM-1) is not actually pinned down: 'exactly one space separates tokens where separation is required' leaves required-ness undefined (is 'iadd.wrap<T>(x,y)' or 'iadd.wrap<T>( x , y )' canonical?), and there is no rule for line breaks — GRAM-3's fn_decl production itself spans lines with an indentation/wrapping convention no rule licenses. Two conforming implementations will disagree on canonical bytes, which is a hard error either way (FORM-1), so accepted programs differ per implementation.
findings.fix: Specify the token-separation table (which adjacent token classes take a space) and the newline rules (one statement per line; the declaration-header wrapping rule or a no-wrap rule) as part of FORM-2. Add byte-level golden tests to spec CI.

findings.problem: STRING is used by the grammar (doc STRING, check ... trap STRING) but FORM-5's exhaustive literal list ('No other literal forms') excludes strings, and STRING's lexical form — quoting, escapes, permitted characters — is defined nowhere. This is both an internal contradiction and an undefined byte-canonical surface (are escapes normalized? is \u allowed?).
findings.fix: Add STRING to FORM-5 with a complete lexical definition (delimiter, escape set, canonical escape policy so each string value has exactly one spelling), or replace strings in doc/trap with a defined restricted form.

findings.problem: Two gaps: (a) 'slice<T> (view; always borrowed, never owned)' is a type-specific exception to the uniform mode rule OWN-2 (an implicit exception list, META-3) and leaves undefined what region a slice-typed struct field or return value carries — a stored view is precisely the escape case OWN-4 polices, but slice has no region parameter to check. (b) 'N a const generic' introduces const generics whose argument forms and relationship to FN-2's 'explicit type arguments' are defined nowhere.
findings.fix: Give slice an explicit region parameter (slice<'r, T>) so ownership/escape rules apply uniformly with no exception text, and add one rule defining const-generic arguments (integer literals only in v0, spelled in targs).

findings.problem: Kernel-reserve pricing is incomplete against decided law: round-4 requires the kernel reserve — 'D1a-simplified ownership calculus, obligation ledger, FFI frames, capability predicates' — to be priced FIRST (round4-synthesis-teachability.md line 19; decision-gates.md line 84), but SCOPE-1 scopes the gated family/FFI frames out of this artifact and Send/Sync-class capability predicates (writer-visible in signatures per round-2 contract kernel, round2-synthesis line 32) appear nowhere. The calibration audit therefore prices only part of the reserve while claiming the baseline role.
findings.fix: Either add the writer-visible reserve items (capability predicates; the writer-visible shape of frames/ledger references) to this artifact, or record explicitly in the audit and decision-gates that the v0 baseline covers only the writer-facing subset and the reserve pricing gate remains open.

findings.problem: Stated-once violations that the spec's own CI rule would fail: trap=abort is stated three times (SCOPE-3, EFF-3, ERR-1); proven-else-checked three times (SCOPE-2, OP-4, OP-5); match-as-the-only-conditional/dispatch four times (GRAM-5, OP-6, FN-5, ERR-1); gated-family-not-writer-writable twice (SCOPE-1, PROG-1); the check statement's spelling twice (GRAM-4, OP-5); and OWN-9 restates OWN-1/OWN-5 consequences as fresh normative text. Near-duplicate detection (round4-synthesis line 34) fails on all of these.
findings.fix: Keep one normative statement per fact (trap semantics in SCOPE-3; proven-else-checked in SCOPE-2; dispatch in GRAM-5) and convert the other mentions to bare rule-ID cross-references; recast OWN-9 as an explicitly derived, non-normative consequences note or delete it.

findings.problem: 'index places overlap conservatively unless indices are distinct constants' is exception-clause phrasing (META-3) rather than a total definition.
findings.fix: Restate as one total rule: 'two index places with the same base overlap iff their index expressions are not both constants with unequal values; all other prefix-related places overlap.'

findings.problem: Wrong cross-reference: 'Documentation is the doc field of declarations (§4)' — §4 is Types; the doc production lives in §3 (GRAM-3).
findings.fix: Change the citation to §3/GRAM-3, and prefer rule-ID citations over section numbers throughout (META-4 uses rule IDs).

findings.problem: The token 'unit' is simultaneously a type (TYPE-1) and a value literal (FORM-5) — one spelling, two constructs, resolved by position, which brushes against the zero-context-dependence invariant's 'no positionally-resolved meaning' (round4-synthesis line 32) even though each occurrence is production-local.
findings.fix: Split the spellings (e.g. type unit, literal unit_v) or add an explicit note-rule that type and literal namespaces are disjoint grammar positions so the resolution is production-local, not contextual.

findings.problem: The parenthetical '(Flow-sensitive shortening is a permitted future refinement; ...)' embeds a future-amendment permission inside a normative rule — proto-exception text that belongs in the decision gates, not in the counted artifact (it is spec mass that teaches the writer nothing).
findings.fix: Delete the parenthetical; record the NLL-refinement option in decision-gates.md (it is already there under the D1a checker levers, decision-gates.md line 74).

findings.problem: The audited baseline '59 rules' (decision-gates.md line 90; notes/calibration-audit-v0.json) counts rule-ID occurrences, including the two cross-reference citations ([GRAM-1] cited in META-1, [SCOPE-3] cited in OP-4); the artifact defines 57 unique rules. The spec-delta ratchet (round4-synthesis line 36) starts from a miscounted baseline, and cross-references will inflate future counts — exactly the individuation gaming the round-4 open tension warns about.
findings.fix: Recount using definition-position IDs only (57), update decision-gates.md and calibration-audit-v0.json, and fold 'citations do not count as rules' into the pending canonical rule-individuation procedure.

findings.problem: Rule-to-emitter bijection is already broken at prototype scale: the checker emits TYPE-5 for an unknown binding (prototype/checker/checker.py line 198), but TYPE-5 is about type inference — the spec contains no name-resolution/scoping rule at all, so the checker had to misappropriate an ID. Round-4's closed-diagnostic-vocabulary invariant requires every rejection to cite a rule whose canonical text actually covers it (round4-synthesis line 40).
findings.fix: Add a scoping/definition-before-use rule (see missing rules) and retarget the checker's diagnostic; add the rule-to-emitter coverage audit to spec CI now, while there is exactly one emitter.

missingRules: Copy class rule: which types copy on use vs move under affine ownership (currently only a comment in prototype/checker/checker.py line 19); without it OWN-1 makes all primitive reuse illegal.
Dereference/access-through-borrow: a place production for deref plus the OWN rule licensing reads via any borrow and writes only via &mut; borrows are otherwise unusable.
Match ownership rule: scrutinee move-vs-borrow semantics and arm-binder modes (owned payload moves vs region-inheriting borrows), locally decidable per OWN-2's no-default stance.
Aggregate construction: the canonical call-shaped spelling for struct values and enum variants, with explicit type arguments; no expr form currently constructs any composite.
Conformance declaration item: grammar production for declaring (type, contract) conformance plus the coherence rule FN-3 asserts; also the generics production for bounds (<T: Monoid>).
Name resolution/scoping rule: definition-before-use, shadowing legality, and duplicate-name rejection, with its own rule ID (the prototype checker already needs one and misuses TYPE-5).

Canonical operation table: complete closed inventory of kernel operations with signatures, mode availability per row, and effect rows — replacing OP-2/OP-3's 'likewise'/'etc.' prose and absorbing the idiv mode restriction as table data (round-2 typed-operation-table floor).

Prelude inventory: definitions of Bool, Result<T,E>, Option<T>, Overflow (and the idiv error type), plus full signatures with effect rows for index, alloc_arena, alloc_heap, band/bor/bnot, ieq/ilt/ile, cvt; FN-1 makes signatures the unit of trust but no prelude signature is stated.
Index type rule: the integer type of array/slice indices and of the const generic N (no usize exists in TYPE-1).

Effect-row grammar: spelling, separators, and one canonical ordering for rows; definition of which allocations count for allocates(...) (stack exemption stated as definition, not exception); the row-subsumption relation PROG-2 uses, or its removal.
REGIONID lexical form and region arguments at call sites: explicit spelling for supplying caller regions at calls (no-inference law forbids inferring them); definition or deletion of 'static.

Caller-region ordering rule: distinct caller-supplied regions are incomparable and order-requiring checks fail closed (the prototype currently invents declaration-order outlives, checker.py lines 65-71).
STRING lexical definition: delimiters, escape set, and canonical escape policy so every string value has exactly one byte spelling.
Line-break/statement-layout rules completing FORM-2: token-separation table and newline placement (including the declaration-header wrapping rule GRAM-3's own formatting presupposes).

D1-critical fact-class enumeration: which checkable fact classes obligate proven-else-checked (bounds; alias-disjointness where a check form exists) and the explicit statement that overflow is a policy class outside it (imports round-3 layer assignment into the counted artifact).

Writer-facing schemas (no-shadow-spec perimeter): trap-report schema, machine diagnostics vocabulary and node-path format, site-enumerated edit-list schema, and the elaborated re-read artifact format, inside the counted artifact.
Send/Sync-class capability predicates: the writer-visible kernel-reserve item (round-2 contract kernel; round-4 kernel-reserve pricing) absent from the artifact even as a named placeholder.
Entry-point rule: existence and uniqueness of main (PROG-2 constrains its signature but nothing requires exactly one).

Conservative-extension statement: one sentence making unknown constructs deterministic hard errors at kernel extension points (round-4 retained law; currently only implied by FORM-1's canonical-form error).

findings.problem: Outlives direction is INVERTED. OWN-4 permits a store/return 'unless the destination's declared region outlives-or-equals 'a'. Requiring dest-outlives-borrow accepts exactly the dangerous case (storing an inner-region borrow into an outer-lived destination -> dangling on region exit) and rejects the safe one (storing a long borrow into a short-lived slot, standard &'long T <: &'short T subtyping). The prototype implements the OPPOSITE, correct direction: checker.py line 174 requires outlives(r, dest_r) (borrow region outlives destination), and test_escape_inner_region_to_outer (test_checker.py:80) rejects inner->outer, which the spec text as written would accept. Spec letter is unsound AND diverges from the passing prototype.
findings.fix: Rewrite OWN-4: 'A borrow &'a p may be stored into a destination of declared region 'b, or returned into return region 'b, only if 'a outlives-or-equals 'b.'

findings.problem: No rule ties a borrow's region to the BORROWED PLACE'S storage duration. OWN-3/OWN-4 only relate regions to regions and borrows to destinations; nothing forbids borrowing a stack-frame local (or owned parameter) at a caller-supplied region. Concrete dangling reference: fn f(x: own T) regions['r0] { return &r0 x; } — x's frame storage dies at return, borrow survives. The prototype not only misses this, it ENSHRINES it as a positive test: test_return_caller_region_borrow (test_checker.py:180) accepts returning &r0 of an own parameter; checker.py's borrow path (line 158-183) checks require_live and region existence but never compares the binding's depth/scope against the borrow's region. This is the core soundness rule of every region calculus (Tofte-Talpin, Cyclone — both uncarded per missing-research-backlog.jsonl item 68) and it is absent, directly violating directive D1 (use-after-free impossible, user-directives.md).
findings.fix: Add rule (proposed OWN-10): a borrow &'a p is legal only if p's storage provably outlives 'a — for an own binding, 'a must be introduced within that binding's scope (so never a caller region for locals/own params); for a place reached through a borrow &'b q, require 'b outlives-or-equals 'a. Fix the prototype test to expect rejection.

findings.problem: Overlap is purely syntactic (prefix in the place grammar), but places can be rooted at borrow-holder bindings, and those alias the borrowed place while never overlapping it syntactically. Concrete hole: let p: &mut 'a X = &mut 'a x; let r: &'a _ = &'a p.f; — place (p,f) shares no prefix with (x), so OWN-5/OWN-7 see no conflict, yet r is a live readable alias of x.f while p can write x.f. I confirmed the prototype accepts this construction (places_overlap((x,),(p,f)) is False at checker.py:166; no test covers it). This breaks OWN-9's exported guarantee ('an &mut borrow does not alias any other live access path'), which per corpus verified-findings.md F3/F4 becomes LLVM noalias-class facts whose violation is UB — i.e., real miscompilation, not just a checker gap. Same root cause makes slice-view aliasing invisible (cf. phase2-arrays-findings.jsonl A001: MemRef views alias original storage and need verifier obligations).
findings.fix: Add a place-resolution rule: a place rooted at a ref-mode binding denotes the borrowed place with the path appended (deref-transparent places); OWN-5 and OWN-7 overlap are judged on RESOLVED places. Define 'holder' normatively (the binding bound to a borrow expression) since OWN-5's 'through that borrow' is currently undefined. Forbid borrowing a binding that holds a live &mut.

findings.problem: The access restrictions apply only to 'p itself', not to overlapping places. While &mut 'a x.f is live, the letter forbids reading/writing/moving x.f — but 'use x' and 'set x = v' (the parent place, which contains f) are neither 'p itself' nor borrows, so the text permits them; likewise 'set x = v' is permitted while &'a x.f is live (only 'p may not be... written' is stated, p = x.f). Both overwrite or read exclusively-borrowed memory. The prototype correctly rejects both via overlap checks in require_readable/require_writable (checker.py:203-222), so the spec letter is both unsound and divergent from the checker that passed the D1a gate (checker-prototype-report.json claims OWN-5 covered — it covers the checker's stronger, unwritten rule).
findings.fix: Restate OWN-5 over overlapping places: 'while an &mut 'a p is live, no place overlapping p may be read, written, moved, or borrowed other than through the holder of that borrow; while any &'a p is live, no place overlapping p may be written, moved, or mutably borrowed.'

findings.problem: The clause 'p itself may not be read, written, or moved EXCEPT THROUGH THAT BORROW' textually licenses moving owned content out through an &mut borrow: region 'a { let p = &mut 'a x; let y: own T = move p; } — the move is 'through that borrow', so OWN-5 permits it; x's binding remains live, so after the region the owner is used/dropped again -> use-after-move/double-drop (D1 violation). The prototype forbids exactly this under OWN-1 ('move through non-owning binding', checker.py:154-155, test_move_through_borrow_binding), a rule that appears nowhere in the spec. Also note the 'except' wording violates META-3 (no exception clauses).
findings.fix: Add an explicit rule: content reached through any borrow (shared or exclusive) may never be moved; moves require a place rooted at an own-mode binding. Reword OWN-5 to grant read/write-through-holder positively rather than as an exception clause.

findings.problem: No copy/move classification exists and the grammar cannot express the distinction. OWN-1 makes EVERY value affine ('a value is moved at most once'), so iadd.wrap<i32>(x, x) is literally a double move of x, and any second read of any binding is a use-after-move. The prototype survives only because its toy AST has separate 'use' and 'move' node kinds (checker.py header, lines 19-21) — but GRAM-4's expr production is 'literal | place | call | match_expr | borrow_expr' with no move spelling, so the canonical grammar cannot represent the distinction the calculus depends on, breaking GRAM-1's grammar/core-tree isomorphism. Whether shared borrows and primitives are duplicable is likewise unstated; if &mut borrow values are copyable, two live &mut aliases exist and OWN-9 is unsound.
findings.fix: Add rules: (a) a copy class — primitives (TYPE-1) and shared borrows are copied on use; own-mode composites and &mut borrows are affine; (b) an explicit 'move p' expression spelling in GRAM-4 (one spelling per construct, satisfying FORM-1/D2 regularity); a bare place expression of affine type is a hard error.

findings.problem: Loop back-edges are unhandled. OWN-6 defines liveness as 'creation to the end of the enclosing region block', and loop bodies are not region blocks (GRAM-4), so a borrow created inside 'loop l { let p = &mut 'a x; ... }' where 'a encloses the loop is still live when the back-edge re-executes the borrow: iteration 2 creates a second &mut 'a x while iteration 1's is live — two live exclusive borrows, violating the OWN-9 noalias export — yet a single-pass lexical checker sees one textual borrow and accepts. Same for moves: 'move x' of a loop-external binding is a runtime use-after-move on iteration 2. Nothing in OWN-1..9 mentions loops; the prototype has no loop support at all (check_stmt raises GRAM-4 'unknown stmt kind'; checker-prototype-report.json lists 'match/loop control flow interaction with liveness' as not covered), so this hole is currently invisible to the gate evidence.
findings.fix: Add a loop rule: inside 'loop l', (a) a borrow may only name a region whose region block is itself inside l's body (forcing 'loop l { region 'b { ... } }' for per-iteration borrows), and (b) a binding declared outside l may not be moved inside l (copies exempt).

findings.problem: Arena escape is not locally decidable, contradicting META-2 and leaving STOR-4 unenforceable. 'let x: own T = alloc_arena<'a, T>(v);' produces a binding whose declared mode/type ('own T') carries no trace of 'a; after 'let y: own T = move x;' and a move past the end of 'a, the value dangles. STOR-4 says this 'is an OWN-4 violation', but OWN-4 is a rule about borrows and declared regions — an arena-owned value has neither, so no rule can mechanically fire at any node given only declared types in scope (META-2). A checker would need whole-function storage-class dataflow the spec never licenses. The prototype has no STOR coverage (report: 'storage contracts (STOR)' not covered), so this is untested.
findings.fix: Surface the region in the type: alloc_arena<'a, T> returns a distinct type (e.g. own<'a> T or arena<'a, T>), moves preserve it, and OWN-4's outlives machinery then applies to arena-owned moves/stores exactly as to borrows.

findings.problem: Borrows cannot be returned or stored per the grammar, yet OWN-4 legislates both. fn_decl returns '-> type' with no mode slot, and 'field := IDENT ":" type' likewise; TYPE-2's type list has no borrow/reference type former (&r T exists only as a binding/param MODE). So OWN-4's 'stored into a value or returned' is grammatically inexpressible, while the prototype's positive test test_return_caller_region_borrow returns one. Also slice<T> ('always borrowed') can then never be a field or return type. Spec-internal contradiction guaranteeing writer/checker divergence.
findings.fix: Decide and state: either add a borrow type former (&'r T as a type, usable in return position and struct fields with declared regions), or restrict OWN-4 to let-bindings and forbid borrow returns/fields outright and delete the return clause. The prototype and OWN-4 both assume the former.

findings.problem: slice<T> is a borrow with no region and no birth. It has no region parameter (so OWN-3/OWN-4/OWN-6 cannot bound its life), no creation operation anywhere in GRAM-4/OP (only 'index' consumes one), and no OWN-7 overlap story: two slices s, t viewing one array are distinct place bases, so index(s,i) vs index(t,j) never overlap syntactically — if two &mut-mode slices are ever constructible the OWN-9 noalias export is silently false (the task-relevant miscompile class per verified-findings.md F3/F4; cf. phase2-arrays-findings.jsonl A001 on view aliasing obligations, A008 on Futhark uniqueness for views). Report lists no slice coverage in the prototype.
findings.fix: Make it slice<'a, T>; add exactly one creation operation (e.g. slice<'a, T>(place, lo, hi)) specified as a borrow of the underlying array place for OWN-5/OWN-7 purposes; state that two slice places over the same resolved root overlap conservatively.

findings.problem: The OWN rules say nothing about CALLS passing borrows. Unspecified: (a) how caller regions instantiate callee region_params at a call site (no substitution rule exists; OWN-3's 'caller-supplied region outlives every local region' is an assumption the spec never obliges call sites to validate, e.g. that passed regions are live at the call); (b) that argument-position borrows are live across (and per OWN-6, well past) the call for OWN-5 purposes; (c) that a callee's effect row (EFF-1 reads('r)/writes('r)) instantiated at actuals counts as caller-side reads/writes for OWN-5 — without this, calling a writes('a) function while an &'a shared borrow is live is unrejectable and breaks OWN-9; (d) disjointness of resolved places for two &mut-mode arguments when pre-existing borrow bindings are passed; (e) region instantiation of a returned borrow. FN-1 says 'callers rely only on signatures' but no rule consumes the signature's modes/regions/effects at the call node. The prototype's call case ignores everything (checker.py:185-188 just recurses into args).
findings.fix: Add an OWN-CALL rule cluster: region-argument substitution at call nodes; argument borrows treated as live accesses of their resolved places for the call; callee effect row instantiated and checked against live borrows under OWN-5; returned-borrow region = instantiated actual, then OWN-4 applies.

findings.problem: Match binders carry no written mode — 'arm := TYPEID "(" binder_list? ")"' — directly contradicting OWN-2 ('Modes are always written; there is no default'). Also unstated: whether matching an own-mode scrutinee moves it (and whether binders move payload fields = partial move), whether one can match through a borrow, and the binding-state JOIN across arms (a binding moved in one arm but not another: live or dead after the match?). ERR-2 forces exhaustive matches everywhere (it is the only conditional, per GRAM-5), so every conditional in the language hits this hole. Prototype: no match support (report not_yet_covered).
findings.fix: Require written modes on binders; state scrutinee semantics (v0-simple: matching an own place moves it; matching through &/&mut yields binders of the same borrow mode and region); add the conservative join: a binding moved in ANY arm is dead after the match.

findings.problem: Partial moves conflate place and binding: 'a value is moved at most once; after a move the SOURCE BINDING is dead' — but moves target places (move x.f). Whether moving x.f kills all of x (prototype behavior: Binding.state='moved' is whole-binding, checker.py:156) or only x.f (requiring per-place deadness and partial-drop elaboration in the DIAG-2 artifact) is unstated; each choice changes which programs are accepted and what drops elaborate.
findings.fix: State the v0 rule explicitly: moving any place rooted at binding b kills b entirely; reads of disjoint sibling fields after a partial move are OWN-1 errors requiring restructuring (consistent with OWN-8 posture).

findings.problem: Lexical liveness plus no reborrow rule makes sequential &mut use in one region impossible, a guaranteed high-frequency AI-writer rejection: f(&mut 'a x); g(&mut 'a x); — the first argument borrow is live until the region block ends (OWN-6), so the second violates OWN-5. Every mutating call needs its own nested region block. That may be the intended D1a trade (Austral-style restrictiveness, corpus K001), but the spec never says so, and the writer-facing idiom appears nowhere; checker-prototype-report.json's own caveat flags 'rejection rates on realistic AI-written code unmeasured'. Divergence between what the spec teaches and what the checker accepts is exactly the D2 spec-as-teacher failure mode.
findings.fix: Either add a call-scoped borrow rule (an argument borrow not bound by let dies at the end of the call expression — a small, flow-free carve-out) or normatively document the region-per-mutating-call idiom in OWN-6/OWN-8 restructuring guidance and include it in the worked-example pack priced by calibration-audit-v0.json.

findings.problem: META-3 ('No rule carries an exception clause') is already violated by the draft it governs: OWN-5 ('except through that borrow'), OWN-7 ('unless indices are distinct constants'), STOR-2 ('unless the initializer is an explicit allocation call'). If META-3 is CI-checked as claimed, spec CI fails on day one; if these are not 'exceptions', the term is undefined.
findings.fix: Restate the three rules in positive form (e.g. OWN-7: 'index places with distinct constant indices are disjoint; all other index pairs overlap') or define 'exception clause' precisely in META-3.

findings.problem: 'Owned values are unaliased' is false while shared borrows of them are live (the owner remains readable under OWN-5, so owner path and borrow path alias for reads). Harmless only because the third bullet makes shared borrows read-only, but an optimizer-facing consequences rule should not require cross-bullet reasoning to be true — and once the OWN-5/OWN-7 holes above are fixed, this sentence should be re-derived, not asserted.
findings.fix: Qualify: 'owned values are unaliased except by live shared borrows, which are read-only for their duration', or drop the bullet and keep only the &mut-noalias and shared-read-only bullets.

findings.problem: Reinitialization after move is unspecified: OWN-1 bans 'use' of a dead binding, but whether 'set x = e' on a moved x is a use (prototype: rejected via require_live in require_writable, checker.py:212) or a revival is unstated. Writers coming from Rust priors will expect revival; the checker will reject.
findings.fix: State it: a moved binding is dead for all statements including set; reinitialization requires a new let (new name, consistent with affine v0).

findings.problem: Region name uniqueness/shadowing is unspecified: nothing forbids 'region a { region a { ... } }' or a local region named identically to a caller region_param. The prototype's index-based region_depth (checker.py:66-67) silently miscomputes outlives under shadowing (index returns the first/outer occurrence).
findings.fix: Add to OWN-3: region identifiers are unique within a function (params included); duplicate introduction is an error.

findings.problem: The grammar is not closed: nonterminals mode, type, type_list, effects, region_params, generics, targs, literal, arg_list, binder_list, fn_sig, law and tokens LABEL, REGIONID, STRING have no productions, so the LL(1)/isomorphism claims (GRAM-1) and the calibration-audit token pricing are over an incomplete artifact; mode's spelling in particular is load-bearing for OWN-2.
findings.fix: Add the missing productions (mode := "own" | "&" REGIONID | "&mut" REGIONID; type := ...; region_params := ...; etc.) before the next audit run.

findings.problem: Non-lexical region exits are unhandled by the elaboration story: 'break LABEL' and 'return' can cross region boundaries (GRAM-4 permits break/return anywhere), but STOR-3/STOR-4 derive drops and arena releases from 'region ends' only, and DIAG-2 requires every derived drop explicit in the artifact — where releases attach on early exit paths is unstated.
findings.fix: State that every control-flow edge leaving a region block (fallthrough, break, return) carries the region's releases/drops in the elaborated artifact, in a defined order (reverse declaration order is the conventional pick — state one).

missingRules: OWN-10 (borrow-storage outlives): a borrow &'a p is legal only if p's storage outlives 'a — own bindings: 'a introduced within the binding's scope; through-borrow places: source borrow's region outlives-or-equals 'a. Closes the dangling-return hole enshrined by test_return_caller_region_borrow.

OWN-11 (place resolution / holders): a place rooted at a ref-mode binding denotes the borrowed place plus the appended path; OWN-5 and OWN-7 are judged on resolved places; 'holder' (the binding bound to a borrow) is defined normatively; borrowing a binding that holds a live &mut is an error.

OWN-12 (copy class + move spelling): primitives and shared borrows copy on use; own composites and &mut borrows are affine; GRAM-4 gains an explicit 'move p' expression; a bare place expression of affine type is an error.

OWN-13 (no move out of borrowed content): content reached through any borrow may never be moved; moves require a place rooted at an own-mode binding (currently only the prototype's unwritten OWN-1 behavior).
OWN-14 (loop back-edge rule): inside a loop, borrows may only name regions introduced inside that loop's body, and bindings declared outside the loop may not be moved inside it (copies exempt).
OWN-15 (match ownership): written modes on arm binders (per OWN-2); scrutinee move/borrow semantics; conservative join — a binding moved in any arm is dead after the match.

OWN-16 (call boundary): region-argument substitution at call nodes; argument borrows are live accesses of resolved places across the call; the callee's effect row instantiated at actuals is checked against live borrows under OWN-5; two &mut-mode arguments must resolve to disjoint places; returned-borrow regions instantiate to actuals and then satisfy OWN-4.

TYPE-6/SLICE (slice regions and birth): slice<'a, T> carries a region; exactly one creation operation specified as a borrow of the underlying array place; slices over the same resolved root overlap conservatively under OWN-7.

STOR-5 (arena values typed by region): alloc_arena<'a, T> returns a region-carrying type so arena escape is locally decidable (META-2) and enforced by OWN-4 machinery, not by the currently un-fireable STOR-4 cross-reference.
OWN-17 (partial moves): moving any place rooted at binding b kills b entirely in v0 (matches prototype whole-binding kill); state it rather than leave 'source binding is dead' ambiguous.
OWN-18 (no reinitialization): set on a moved binding is a use of a dead binding (OWN-1 error); revival requires a new let.

GRAM completion: productions for mode, type, effects, region_params, generics, targs, literal, arg_list, binder_list, plus a borrow type former (or an explicit prohibition) for return types and struct fields so OWN-4's 'stored into a value or returned' is expressible.
STOR-6 (region-exit elaboration): every edge leaving a region block (fallthrough, break, return) carries that region's drops/arena releases in the DIAG-2 artifact, in a stated order.

Process note (per checker-prototype-report.json caveat and missing-research-backlog.jsonl item 68): cross-check the repaired calculus against Featherweight Rust / Oxide / Austral formalizations before ratification — the two inverted/exception-clause bugs found here (OWN-4 direction, OWN-5 'except through that borrow') are exactly the class a formal-core comparison catches.

findings.problem: The prelude is normative but undefined. GRAM-5 requires 'enum Bool { True(); False(); }' "in the prelude", ERR-1 makes Result<T,E>/Option<T> "prelude enums", OP-2 returns Result<T, Overflow>, PROG-1 closes the world "plus the prelude" — yet no rule anywhere defines the prelude's contents, variant names (Ok/Err? Some/None?), or the operation signature table (generics, operand/result types, effect rows of iadd.*, cvt, index, band...). A writer cannot emit a single exhaustive match (ERR-2) over Result without guessing variant names; nothing in the corpus fixes them either (grep for Ok(/Err(/Some(/Overflow across optimizer-language-research/debates/*.md finds only round3-synthesis-safety-envelope.md line 21's policy use of 'Overflow'). This violates the no-shadow-spec rule adopted as decided law in round4-synthesis-teachability.md ('Decided now', no-shadow-spec: all normative writer-facing content counts inside the one artifact) and falsifies SCOPE-1's claim that the document contains everything the writer needs. notes/calibration-audit-v0.json prices the spec without it.
findings.fix: Add a §Prelude with stable rule IDs: full canonical source of Bool, Result<T,E>, Option<T>, and every error type named by ops (Overflow, division, cvt narrowing), plus a signature table for every §7 operation (name, type parameters, parameter modes/types, return type, effect row). Count it in the calibration audit.

findings.problem: bool is simultaneously a primitive type (TYPE-1), a prelude enum (GRAM-5: 'bool is enum Bool { True(); False(); }'), and has dedicated literals true/false (FORM-5). That is two-or-three spellings for one construct: is a truth value written 'true' or constructed as 'True()'? Is 'bool' or 'Bool' the type name (FORM-3 says lowercase = values, uppercase = types, so 'bool' as a TYPEID is ill-formed)? Can you match on a bool produced by ieq (arms are TYPEID-patterns, but primitives have no variants per ERR-2)? This directly contradicts FORM-1/META-1 and the round-2 one-spelling floor (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md, Minimal core sketch, SURFACE bullet).
findings.fix: Pick one: delete bool from TYPE-1's primitive list, make the type Bool a prelude enum, delete true/false from FORM-5, and state that ieq/ilt/ile and band/bor/bnot return/accept Bool and are dispatched by match True()/False(). If a primitive bool is kept instead, delete GRAM-5's enum claim and add a primitive-condition dispatch rule.

findings.problem: There is no value-construction syntax for any composite type. expr := literal | place | call | match_expr | borrow_expr; no struct literal, no enum-variant construction, no array construction. 'call := path targs? ...' might be meant to cover variant construction, but path is undefined and FORM-3 reserves uppercase for types/variants while call-shaped operations are lowercase. As written, the writer cannot create a Result, an Option, a Bool, an env-struct (FN-5), or any array — making ERR-1 (errors are values, decided law per round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md ERRORS bullet), GRAM-5's Bool dispatch, and FN-5's env-struct pattern all unimplementable.
findings.fix: Add explicit productions and one worked example each: variant_expr := TYPEID targs? "(" arg_list? ")" (e.g., Ok<i32, Overflow>(x)); struct_expr := TYPEID targs? "{" (IDENT ":" expr ";")* "}"; array_expr := "array" targs "(" arg_list? ")". State how targs interact with prelude generics.

findings.problem: Move-vs-copy is undefined, and the grammar cannot express the distinction. OWN-1 says a value is moved at most once, but no rule says which expressions move: is every place-read a move (then iadd.wrap<i32>(x, x) is a double-move error and the language is unusable), or do primitives copy (never stated), or do all reads not move (then reading an owned struct duplicates it and its heap members, giving double-drop/use-after-free — a D1 violation per notes/user-directives.md D1). The prototype had to invent semantics the spec lacks: prototype/checker/checker.py lines 19-21 define separate {"kind":"use"} ('read (copy for prims)') and {"kind":"move"} expression kinds that GRAM-4 collapses into 'place' — writer/checker divergence is already manifest in the project's own code.
findings.fix: Add an OWN rule: primitive types (TYPE-1) are copy; all composite and owned values move on any use as an own-mode operand or initializer; reading a place as a shared-borrow operand never moves. If the core tree distinguishes use/move (as checker.py does), GRAM must too (e.g., explicit 'move p' spelling), preserving GRAM-1's 1:1 production/node isomorphism.

findings.problem: Arena escape is unsound as specified. STOR-4 says escaping an arena value past its region 'is an OWN-4 violation', but OWN-4 governs only borrows (&'a p / &mut 'a p). The result mode/type of alloc_arena<'a, T>(v) is never stated; if it is 'own T' (the only owned mode OWN-2 offers), the value's type carries no region, OWN-1 permits moving/returning owned values, and no rule fires when an arena allocation is returned past region end — use-after-free in an accepted program, violating D1 (notes/user-directives.md) and SCOPE-3. alloc_heap<T>(v)'s return type is equally unstated (TYPE-2 has no box type), so heap-ness is untracked in the type system that STOR-3's drop elaboration needs.
findings.fix: Define the result types: alloc_arena<'a, T>(v) returns &mut 'a T (arena owns; borrow discipline enforces containment via OWN-4), and alloc_heap<T>(v) returns own T with an explicit note that heap vs stack is a storage fact tracked in elaboration, not in the type. Alternatively introduce a region-bound owned mode own<'a> T with its own escape rule — but pick one and give an example.

findings.problem: The optimizer license in EFF-2 ('may deduplicate, reorder, and eliminate' pure calls) is unsound without a termination requirement. pure excludes traps but not divergence (loop with no break is expressible per GRAM-4 and FN-6 permits recursion); eliminating or reordering a diverging pure call changes observable behavior — the classic LLVM forward-progress hazard. This contradicts SCOPE-3 ('accepted programs have no undefined behavior') and the round-1/round-3 verification-spine principle that optimizer facts must be checked, not assumed (round3-synthesis-safety-envelope.md, Layer 0).
findings.fix: Either (a) restrict the license: pure calls may be deduplicated/reordered; elimination of an unused pure call is permitted only with a termination proof; or (b) add divergence to the effect row (diverges) with pure implying termination checked by a conservative structural rule (loops require a break on every path; recursion requires a declared decreasing measure or is rejected in pure fns).

findings.problem: Rows 'checked both ways' makes acceptance prover-relative. traps is exhibited by 'checked ops with unproven facts' (EFF-3) and unproven bounds checks (OP-4), and checks are eliminated 'only by proof'. So whether a body exhibits traps depends on what the checker can prove: upgrade the prover and a previously mandatory 'traps' declaration becomes an unexhibited effect — the same program flips from required-to-declare to rejected-for-declaring. This contradicts round-3 cross-cutting invariant 4 (round3-synthesis-safety-envelope.md line 12: acceptance is artifact-checkable, not process-relative; the accepted-programs quantifier stays stable across toolchain versions).
findings.fix: Define 'exhibits' syntactically, not proof-relatively: any occurrence of a .trap op, check stmt, or bounds-checked index counts as trapping regardless of whether the check is later proven away; or make rows upper bounds (declaring an unexhibited effect is legal, surfaced as a lint in the check report), which also matches PROG-2's 'not exceeding' phrasing.

findings.problem: The spec uses two region spellings, violating its own FORM-1/META-4 and the round-2 one-spelling floor (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md SURFACE bullet). FORM-3 says regions are [a-z][a-z0-9_]* and 'no other identifier forms exist'; GRAM-4 uses an undefined token REGIONID; but OWN-3 writes region 'a, STOR-1 arena<'a>, STOR-2 alloc_arena<'a, T>, EFF-1 reads('r...), and PROG-2 writes('static) all use apostrophe-prefixed forms, which FORM-3 makes ill-formed. A writer model will emit whichever spelling the rule it read used, and the checker will implement the other (prototype/checker/test_checker.py already uses bare "a").
findings.fix: Pick one spelling, define REGIONID as a token (either 'IDENT-with-apostrophe-prefix' or plain IDENT in region position), and rewrite every occurrence in OWN-3, OWN-4, STOR-1, STOR-2, EFF-1, PROG-2 to it. Run the META-4 duplicate/near-duplicate CI check the spec promises.

findings.problem: STOR-2 contradicts STOR-1 and decided law. STOR-1: 'Every allocation names its storage class explicitly.' STOR-2: bindings 'are stack unless the initializer is an explicit allocation call' — a default with an 'unless' clause, i.e., exactly the exception form META-3 bans, and a violation of the round-2 memory floor 'every allocation carries a writer-stated, compiler-checked storage/lifetime contract' (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md MEMORY bullet, reaffirmed as mandatory-on-every-allocation in round4-synthesis-teachability.md, Spec architecture bullet 2).
