findings.fix: Either make storage explicit in the grammar (let_stmt := "let" IDENT ":" mode storage type "=" expr ";" with storage := "stack" | "arena" "<" REGIONID ">" | "heap") so STOR-1 is literally true, or rewrite STOR-1/STOR-2 as one uniform positive rule ('a let binding's storage class is stack; arena/heap storage exists only via alloc_arena/alloc_heap, whose names are the explicit statement') with no 'unless'.

findings.problem: Conformance is undeclarable. FN-3: 'Conformance is declared explicitly per (type, contract) and checked per member' — but GRAM-2's item := fn_decl | struct_decl | enum_decl | contract_decl contains no conformance/impl item, and no production anywhere binds (type, contract) to member implementations. The contract-group kernel is decided-required machinery ('required under every open outcome', round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md CONTRACT KERNEL bullet), and FN-2/FN-3 generics are unusable without it: a writer cannot make any type satisfy T: Monoid, so no constrained generic can ever be instantiated.
findings.fix: Add item and production, e.g., conform_decl := "conform" TYPEID ":" TYPEID "{" doc? fn_decl* "}" (type : contract, members are full fn_decls checked against the contract's fn_sigs), plus the at-most-one-conformance coherence rule as its own ID and a worked example (Monoid for i32).

findings.problem: The grammar references 15 nonterminals and 3 token classes it never defines: type, mode, generics, targs, param_list, type_list, arg_list, binder_list, path, fn_sig, law, effects, region_params, expr_stmt, literal; tokens STRING, LABEL, REGIONID. SCOPE-2 gates acceptance on 'parses under the canonical grammar', which is therefore undefined. Worst holes: no type production at all (how to write Pair<i32, i64>, array<u8, 16>, a borrow-typed field &r T, or a region-parameterized struct — needed by OWN-4's 'stored into a value'); no mode production despite OWN-2; no rule for how call sites pass region arguments or const-generic arguments (FN-2 demands explicit args); STRING is used by doc and check yet FORM-5 says 'no other literal forms', and string escape rules are alternate-spelling hazards under FORM-1.
findings.fix: Add every missing production and token definition, including: type := PRIM | TYPEID targs? | "array" "<" type "," const ">" | "slice" "<" type ">" | "&" REGIONID type | "&mut" REGIONID type; mode := "own" | "&" REGIONID | "&mut" REGIONID; generics := "<" gparam ("," gparam)* ">" with gparam covering T, T: Contract, const N, and regions; targs covering types, regions, and const literals (state whether const args carry FORM-5 suffixes); expr_stmt := expr ";"; STRING with a closed escape set; LABEL and REGIONID charsets.

findings.problem: match_expr cannot produce a value: expr includes match_expr, but arm bodies are '{" stmt* "}' with no result expression, no tail position, no '=> expr' form. Since there is no 'if' (GRAM-5) and let requires an initializer (GRAM-4), conditional initialization — the single most common pattern an AI writer needs (let y = if c then a else b) — is inexpressible: match-as-expr yields nothing and match-as-stmt cannot initialize an immutable binding. GRAM-5's claim that Bool 'is dispatched by match' is unusable in expression position.
findings.fix: Either add a value form (arm := TYPEID "(" binder_list? ")" "=>" expr ";" when match is used as an expr, keeping the stmt-arm form for stmt position — at the cost of two arm shapes), or remove match from expr, make it a statement only, and add the canonical idiom as a worked example (declare with let + placeholder is impossible, so likely: each arm ends 'return' or 'set' into a pre-bound own binding — which itself needs an uninitialized-let or default rule that does not currently exist).

findings.problem: Match binder ownership is undefined. arm := TYPEID "(" binder_list? ")" — binder_list has no production, and no OWN rule says whether binders move the payload out of the scrutinee, copy it, or borrow it; nothing says what binders do when the scrutinee is a borrow (&r Option<T>): binding the payload by value through a shared borrow would move out of a borrowed place (an OWN-5 violation) — so borrowed-scrutinee matching presumably binds by borrow, but that is nowhere stated and the binder then needs a region. Rust required a dedicated design (match ergonomics) here; a model will guess and diverge from the checker.
findings.fix: Add a rule: matching an own scrutinee moves it and binders are own; matching &r e / &mut r e gives binders of mode &r / &mut r respectively; binders always state nothing (modes derived from scrutinee mode — one uniform rule), with one worked example of each.

findings.problem: The LL(1) claim is false for the grammar as written: (a) place := IDENT | place "." IDENT | ... is left-recursive — no left-recursive grammar is LL(1); (b) expr can start with either a place (x.f) or a call whose path is dotted (iadd.wrap<T>(x,y)), so after IDENT '.' IDENT the parser cannot decide place-vs-call within one token; (c) 'index' is both a place-keyword (GRAM-4) and a FORM-3-legal identifier, and no reserved-word list exists — a writer may legally name a binding 'index', 'set', 'loop', or 'match' and produce unparseable-or-ambiguous text.
findings.fix: Rewrite place iteratively (place := IDENT suffix*; suffix := "." IDENT | ...), resolve the call/place prefix by making operation names single tokens (see the OP-2 finding) or by requiring targs/'(' lookahead to be part of a factored production, and add a rule enumerating reserved words excluded from IDENT.

findings.problem: Dotted operation names (iadd.wrap, fadd.strict) have no lexical home: FORM-3 identifiers cannot contain '.', 'path' is undefined, and '.' already means field projection in places, creating the GRAM-1 ambiguity. Also underspecified: does ineg have all three modes ('likewise' scope is ambiguous — negating i32::MIN overflows, so it matters); idiv overflow (INT_MIN/-1) is unmentioned (only divisor-zero); and whether ige/igt/ine exist or writers must compose ile/ilt/bnot with swapped args is unstated.
findings.fix: Define opname as a single token class (e.g., OPNAME := [a-z]+ "." [a-z]+) distinct from IDENT, or respell modes as suffixed names (iadd_wrap) fitting FORM-3. Enumerate the full op×mode matrix in a table (including ineg modes and idiv's INT_MIN/-1 behavior per mode) and state the comparison-op closure rule (only ieq/ilt/ile exist; others are compositions).

findings.problem: Borrow liveness endpoint is ambiguous: OWN-6 says 'from its creation to the end of the enclosing region block' while OWN-4 says the borrow 'names the region whose scope bounds the borrow's life'. For &'a p written inside an inner region block where 'a is caller-supplied, these give different answers (inner block end vs whole function). prototype/checker/checker.py lines 124-125 implements the named-region reading (borrows die when their NAMED region exits), meaning a temporary &'a x passed as a call argument locks x for the entire function — a semantics a writer must be taught explicitly (name the innermost region for temporaries) or every second program will be rejected. The lifetime of borrows not bound to any binding (call-argument temporaries) is not addressed by any rule.
findings.fix: Rewrite OWN-6: 'a borrow is live from its creation to the end of the block of its NAMED region', delete 'enclosing', add a rule or note that call-argument temporaries should name the innermost enclosing region, and add a canonical example of both the rejected (outer-region temporary) and accepted (inner-region temporary) forms.

findings.problem: The effect-row surface is undefined at the byte level and semantically incomplete: (a) no grammar — separator, ordering canon (PROG-2 shows writes, allocates, traps in that order but nothing legislates it, so two orderings = two spellings, violating FORM-1), empty-row spelling vs 'pure'; (b) is stack allocation an effect? Every fn with a let allocates a frame — if allocates(stack) is required, virtually every function carries it; if exempt, the exemption is an unstated special case (META-3 risk); (c) how rows compose at call sites (callee's reads('r) substituted through region args) is unstated; (d) PROG-2's 'not exceeding' presumes a row-subsumption relation EFF-1's exactness doctrine never defines. Round-3 flags the effect-row floor as having zero exemplar cards (round3-proof-burden.md, Weaknesses/experiments), so the spec cannot lean on precedent — it must state everything.
findings.fix: Add effects := effect ("," effect)* with a fixed canonical order (reads < writes < allocates < traps), define pure as the empty row's unique spelling, state 'stack frames are not allocations for EFF purposes' as a positive rule, define call-site row substitution, and define row subsumption once for PROG-2.

findings.problem: writes('static) references a region 'static that no rule introduces: OWN-3 defines only lexical regions and caller-supplied region params; there are no global/static declarations in GRAM-2 (item has no static/const item), so nothing can live in 'static and the permission is a grant over an inexpressible effect. It is also spelled with the apostrophe form FORM-3 forbids.
findings.fix: Either delete writes('static) from PROG-2 (main's row becomes 'allocates(heap), traps' or narrower), or add the static-data story: a 'static region rule in §5 and a static/const item production in GRAM-2 with its storage and initialization semantics.

findings.problem: Zero worked examples exist in the artifact. FORM-1/FORM-2 legislate a single legal byte-level formatting, but no complete program is shown, so the canonical bytes are underdetermined and unlearnable: a model cannot infer where blank lines go, how a fn_decl's effects/region_params wrap across the two lines GRAM-3 itself displays, or what a whole accepted program looks like. Round-4 makes the teaching pack (spec + canonical worked examples) the delivery unit (round4-synthesis-teachability.md, Spec architecture bullet 3), and notes/calibration-audit-v0.json explicitly flags 'No worked examples included yet — estimate +2-4k tokens'. For a sole in-context teaching artifact this is the highest-leverage single addition.
findings.fix: Add a §Examples with at minimum: one complete accepted program (main + a generic fn with contract bound + region + borrow + match on Result + check + checked arithmetic), byte-exact, plus per-construct micro-examples for env-struct (FN-5), arena allocation, and enum construction. Mark examples normative for FORM rules.

findings.problem: Byte-level canonical form is underdetermined: FORM-2 fixes indentation and inter-token spacing 'where separation is required' but not comma conventions (f(a, b) vs f(a,b)), whether long expressions ever wrap (fn_decl in GRAM-3 is displayed wrapped across two lines — under what rule?), what counts as a nesting level for match arms and arm bodies, trailing newline at EOF, or blank lines inside bodies. Two writers can both 'comply' and produce different bytes; FORM-1 then makes one of them a hard error chosen by the checker's private convention.
findings.fix: Complete FORM-2 with explicit sub-rules: comma followed by exactly one space, no space before; a declaration/statement occupies exactly one line, no wrapping (unbounded line length) OR a deterministic wrap rule; one nesting level per '{' block including match arms; file ends with exactly one LF; no blank lines inside a block. Then show it in the worked example.

findings.problem: index is doubly specified and incomplete: GRAM-4 makes index a place form with mandatory targs; OP-4 writes index<T>(s, i) as an operation. The index operand's type is never stated (u64? any unsigned? is cvt required first?), the targs arity is unclear for array<T, N> vs slice<T>, and being a place means 'set index<T>(a, i) = e' is a write — its interaction with OWN-7's conservative overlap and OWN-5 exclusivity for non-constant indices deserves its own statement. Moreover, no operation creates a slice from an array (slices are 'always borrowed' per TYPE-2, but there is no slice-of/borrow-to-slice op) and no length operation exists, so slices are unconstructible and loops over arrays cannot terminate on a queried bound.
findings.fix: Specify: index<T>(s, i) with i: u64 (one rule, no alternatives), targs = element type only; add slice<T> creation (e.g., slice_all<'a, T, N>(&'a arr) -> slice<T> and slice_range with checked bounds) and len<T>(s) -> u64; state that index-as-place is the sole lvalue form and cross-reference OWN-7 for overlap.

findings.problem: 'fadd.strict<T> etc.' — 'etc.' is not normative. The float op set (fsub/fmul/fdiv/fneg? fma? sqrt?) is unenumerated, float comparisons do not exist anywhere (ieq/ilt/ile are integer ops by name), and NaN semantics for comparison/equality — the classic silent-wrong trap for a generated-code writer — is unstated (is feq total? does NaN != NaN make match-based dispatch on comparison results hazardous?).
findings.fix: Enumerate the closed float op table with modes, add feq/flt/fle<T> with an explicit IEEE-754 NaN rule (all comparisons with NaN yield False; note feq is not an equivalence), and state whether unordered-comparison variants exist (preferably not — one rule).

findings.problem: cvt<i32, i64>(x) is the only conversion example; the conversion matrix is undefined: which (source, target) pairs are value-preserving (widening) vs Result-returning (narrowing, float->int?), what the error type of a failed cvt is (Overflow? a new type?), whether float<->int conversions exist at all, and whether cvt appears in §7's operation inventory (it does not — it lives only in §4, straining META-4's each-fact-once-and-cross-referenced discipline).
findings.fix: Move cvt into §7 as OP-7 with a table: widening int->int and f32->f64 are total; narrowing int->int returns Result<T, Overflow>; int<->float pairs enumerated with their totality/error types; TYPE-4 keeps only the no-implicit-conversion law with a cross-reference to OP-7.

findings.problem: Name-binding rules are missing entirely: nothing legislates declaration-before-use, whether shadowing or redeclaration of a binding is legal, whether loop LABELs share a namespace with values, or label uniqueness/scoping for break. The prototype exposes the gap: prototype/checker/checker.py line 198 raises 'unknown binding' citing TYPE-5 — a rule that is about type inference, not name resolution — because no correct rule ID exists to cite, violating DIAG-1's premise that every rejection cites the governing rule.
findings.fix: Add rules: names must be declared before use; redeclaration/shadowing of a live name in any enclosing scope is an error (one uniform rule — cheapest for a canonical-form language); LABELs and REGIONIDs each have their own namespace with the same no-shadowing rule; break's LABEL must name a lexically enclosing loop.

findings.problem: Return totality and dead code are unspecified: must every control path end in return (or trap)? Does a fn returning unit implicitly return, or must it end 'return unit;'? Is code after return/break rejected as unreachable, accepted, or canonical-form-illegal? Each choice changes what the writer must emit for main() -> unit, and the checker will pick one silently.
findings.fix: Add: every function body must return on all paths ('return unit;' mandatory for unit functions — no implicit return, matching the no-elision doctrine); statements after a return/break in the same block are a hard error citing this rule.

findings.problem: outlives is defined strictly ('strictly encloses'), so 'a never outlives 'a and OWN-4 must patch with 'outlives-or-equals'; meanwhile caller-supplied regions are mutually unordered (nothing says whether a callee may store a borrow of 'a into a destination in 'b), and prototype/checker/checker.py line 71 implements the reflexive relation (<=), quietly diverging from the strict wording.
findings.fix: Define one reflexive relation outlives-or-equals ('a ⊒ 'b iff same region, or 'a's block encloses 'b's block, or 'a is caller-supplied and 'b is local) and use it everywhere; state explicitly that distinct caller-supplied regions are unordered so cross-storing is rejected.

findings.problem: 'p itself may not be read, written, or moved except through that borrow' — 'through that borrow' is undefined: the checker must identify which access paths count as through-the-borrow (prototype/checker/checker.py lines 207, 215-217 approximates this as 'the binding holding the borrow', a notion — holder — that no spec rule introduces).
findings.fix: Add a definition: an access is 'through' a borrow iff its place's base is the binding whose initializer created that borrow; make holder-binding a defined term in §5.

findings.problem: law has no production (contract_decl shows law* but no law := ...) and no vocabulary: are law names (associative, commutative, ...) a closed set the law-test harness understands, or free-form? What arities/argument forms are legal? A writer cannot emit a law without guessing.
findings.fix: Add law := "law" LAWNAME "(" IDENT ("," IDENT)* ")" ";" with a closed LAWNAME table (associative, commutative, identity, idempotent...) each with its arity and the checked property, stated once.

findings.problem: 'polymorphic recursion is not [permitted] (instantiation closure must be finite)' names a property, not a decidable rule: the writer cannot predict which recursive generic patterns are rejected, and no rule ID exists for the diagnostic to cite the check procedure.
findings.fix: State the conservative decidable rule (e.g., in any call cycle, every generic function must be called at exactly its own type parameters — syntactic check) so rejection is predictable and locally explainable.

findings.problem: The kernel is silently concurrency-free: no spawn/thread/atomic constructs exist, so D1's data-race half (notes/user-directives.md D1) holds vacuously, and the Send/Sync-class capability predicates that round-4 places inside the kernel reserve to be priced first (round4-synthesis-teachability.md, Decided now: 'kernel reserve is priced FIRST: ... capability predicates') are absent without a stated deferral — the calibration number in notes/calibration-audit-v0.json therefore under-prices the reserve.
findings.fix: Add one sentence to SCOPE-1: 'Kernel v0 defines no concurrency constructs; programs are single-threaded, and D1's race-freedom obligation is vacuous until the concurrency kernel (capability predicates) lands as a priced spec delta.' Record the pending delta in the decision gates.

findings.problem: The 'machine-readable trap report' (also EFF-3, OP-5) is writer-facing — the repair loop reads it — but its schema is unspecified and uncounted, which the round-4 no-shadow-spec rule classifies as normative spec mass (round4-synthesis-teachability.md, no-shadow-spec: diagnostics vocabulary and report schemas count). Same applies to ERR-2's site-enumerated edit list.
findings.fix: Either add the trap-report and edit-list schemas to this artifact (a compact field table each) or add an explicit cross-reference stating they are pending counted spec deltas, so the calibration audit's +1-2k estimate becomes a tracked obligation rather than silent shadow spec.

missingRules: PRELUDE section: normative canonical source of Bool, Result<T,E>, Option<T>, and all operation error types (Overflow, division-by-zero, cvt narrowing) with exact variant names, plus a complete signature table for every kernel operation (type params, operand modes/types, return type, effect row) — required by round-4's no-shadow-spec decided law

GRAM productions for: type, mode, generics (type/const/region parameters), targs (type, region, and const arguments at call sites — including whether const args carry FORM-5 suffixes), param_list/type_list/arg_list/binder_list with separator and spacing canon, path (or a single-token OPNAME class replacing dotted paths), fn_sig, law, effects, region_params, expr_stmt, literal; token definitions for STRING (with closed escape set), LABEL, REGIONID; and a reserved-word list excluded from IDENT
Value-construction rules: enum-variant construction, struct construction, and array construction expressions (currently no composite value is constructible)

Copy/move law: which types are copy (primitives), which expressions move, whether a moved binding can be re-initialized via set, and — if the core tree distinguishes use vs move (as prototype/checker/checker.py does) — the corresponding surface spelling to preserve GRAM-1 isomorphism
Match-value rule: expression-valued match (=> expr arms) or the canonical statement idiom for conditional initialization, plus binder ownership modes for own vs &r vs &mut r scrutinees
Conformance declaration item (conform/impl) binding (type, contract) to member implementations, with the one-conformance coherence rule as its own rule ID
Name-resolution rules: declaration-before-use, no shadowing/redeclaration, separate namespaces and scoping for LABELs and REGIONIDs, break-label must name an enclosing loop
Return-totality and dead-code rules: all paths return (return unit; mandatory), statements after return/break are errors

Arena/heap allocation result types: the mode/type returned by alloc_arena<'a,T> (region-bound — e.g., &mut 'a T or an own<'a> T mode) and alloc_heap<T>, closing the STOR-4 escape soundness hole and defining how heap-ness is tracked for drop elaboration
'static region and static-data story (or deletion of writes('static) from PROG-2)

Effect-row completion: row grammar with fixed canonical ordering, pure = empty-row unique spelling, stack-frame exemption from allocates, call-site row substitution through region arguments, and the row-subsumption relation PROG-2's 'not exceeding' presumes
Termination/divergence policy for pure functions (or a restricted EFF-2 elimination license requiring termination proof) — currently an unsoundness
Canonical formatting completion: comma/space rules, line-wrapping (or explicit no-wrap) rule, nesting-depth definition for match arms, EOF newline, blank-line rules inside bodies

Canonical worked examples section: at least one complete byte-exact accepted program plus micro-examples (env-struct pattern, Result match, region+borrow, arena allocation, checked arithmetic) — flagged pending in notes/calibration-audit-v0.json

Slice and array operations: slice creation from arrays, len, and the index operand type (e.g., u64); float operation enumeration with feq/flt/fle and an explicit NaN policy; the full cvt conversion matrix with error types, moved into §7
Explicit kernel-v0 concurrency scope statement (no threads; Send/Sync capability predicates deferred as a priced future spec delta per round-4 kernel-reserve sequencing)

Writer-facing artifact schemas or tracked cross-references: trap report (SCOPE-3/EFF-3), site-enumerated edit list (ERR-2), per-function check/lifetime reports and elaborated-artifact view format (DIAG-2) — all count under the no-shadow-spec rule
Prelude interaction rule: prelude names are in scope in every program and may not be redefined (extends PROG-1's closed world to the prelude boundary)

findings.problem: Outlives direction is INVERTED and unsound (critics B and C, independently, both blocking — no conflict). 'May not be stored/returned unless the destination's region outlives-or-equals 'a' accepts exactly the dangling case (inner-region borrow stored into a longer-lived destination) and rejects the safe direction. The prototype implements the opposite, sound direction (checker.py:170-177 requires the borrow's region to outlive the destination; test_checker.py:80 test_escape_inner_region_to_outer rejects a program the spec text accepts), so spec letter and checker already diverge and the letter violates D1.
findings.fix: Rewrite OWN-4: a borrow &'a p may be stored into a destination of declared region 'b, or returned into return region 'b, only if 'a outlives-or-equals 'b. Add a spec-CI test tying OWN-4's text to the checker's OWN-4 vectors.

findings.problem: No rule ties a borrow's region to the borrowed PLACE'S storage duration (critic C). fn f(x: own T) regions['r0] { return &r0 x; } dangles — x's frame dies at return — yet nothing forbids borrowing a local or own parameter at a caller-supplied region. The prototype enshrines the bug as a POSITIVE test (test_checker.py:180 test_return_caller_region_borrow accepts it); this is the core soundness rule of every region calculus (Tofte-Talpin/Cyclone, both uncarded) and it is absent. Direct D1 violation.
findings.fix: Add OWN-10: &'a p is legal only if p's storage provably outlives 'a — for own bindings, 'a must be introduced within the binding's scope (never a caller region for locals/own params); for places reached through &'b q, require 'b outlives-or-equals 'a. Flip the prototype test to expect rejection.

findings.problem: Exclusivity applies only to 'p itself', not overlapping places (critic C). While &mut 'a x.f is live, the letter permits 'use x' and 'set x = v' (the parent place), and permits 'set x = v' while &'a x.f is live — overwriting/reading exclusively-borrowed memory. The prototype correctly rejects both via overlap checks (checker.py:203-222), so the checker enforces a stronger rule that is written nowhere; the D1a gate evidence covers the unwritten rule, not the spec.
findings.fix: Restate OWN-5 over overlapping places: while &mut 'a p is live, no place overlapping p may be read, written, moved, or borrowed except through the borrow's holder; while any &'a p is live, no overlapping place may be written, moved, or mutably borrowed.

findings.problem: Overlap is purely syntactic, so places rooted at borrow-HOLDER bindings alias the borrowed place invisibly (critic C, blocking): let p = &mut 'a x; then &'a p.f is a live readable alias of x.f while p can write it — accepted by the prototype (places_overlap((x,),(p,f)) is False, checker.py:166, untested). This falsifies OWN-9's exported &mut-noalias guarantee, which becomes LLVM noalias-class UB, i.e. real miscompiles. Related surface gap flagged by critics A and B (both major): the place grammar has no dereference form at all, so through-borrow access — the only legal access under OWN-5 — is inexpressible, and the checker invents holder semantics no rule states; critic D rated the undefined 'holder'/'through that borrow' notion minor. CONFLICT on fix approach: A and B propose an explicit deref(place) production; C proposes deref-transparent resolved places; B warns auto-deref would itself violate the zero-context-dependence law.
findings.fix: Define 'holder' normatively; judge OWN-5/OWN-7 overlap on RESOLVED places (holder-rooted paths resolve to the borrowed place plus appended path); forbid borrowing a binding holding a live &mut. Then pick ONE through-borrow spelling — an explicit deref place production is the safer choice under the no-context-dependence law — with region/exclusivity typing rules in section 5, and align the checker.

findings.problem: 'p itself may not be read, written, or moved EXCEPT THROUGH THAT BORROW' textually licenses moving owned content out through an &mut borrow (critic C): let p = &mut 'a x; let y: own T = move p; leaves x's binding live for a later use/drop — use-after-move/double-drop, a D1 violation. The prototype forbids exactly this under an UNWRITTEN rule (checker.py:154-155, test_move_through_borrow_binding). The 'except' wording also violates META-3.
findings.fix: Add an explicit rule: content reached through any borrow (shared or exclusive) may never be moved; moves require a place rooted at an own-mode binding. Reword OWN-5's grant positively (read/write through the holder) rather than as an exception clause.

findings.problem: No copy/move classification exists and the grammar cannot express the distinction (critics C and D blocking; critic B major — severity conflict noted, resolved to blocking). Under affine OWN-1, iadd.wrap<i32>(x, x) is a double move and any second read of any binding is a use-after-move, making the language unusable; the only 'fix' lives as a comment plus separate use/move node kinds in toolchain code (checker.py:19-21), which GRAM-4's single 'place' production collapses — breaking GRAM-1's grammar/core-tree isomorphism and constituting a hidden exception (META-3) outside the artifact (no-shadow-spec). Whether &mut borrow values are copyable also decides OWN-9's soundness (copyable &mut = two live exclusive aliases).
findings.fix: Add one uniform rule: TYPE-1 primitives and shared borrows copy on use; own-mode composites and &mut borrows are affine. Add an explicit 'move p' expression to GRAM-4; a bare place expression of affine type is a hard error. Align the checker.

findings.problem: Loop back-edges are unhandled (critic C). Loop bodies are not region blocks, so a borrow created inside 'loop' against an enclosing region is still live when the back-edge re-executes it — two live &mut borrows of one place (OWN-9/noalias violated) — and 'move x' of a loop-external binding is a runtime use-after-move on iteration 2. No OWN rule mentions loops; the prototype has zero loop support (check_stmt raises on loops; checker-prototype-report.json lists loop/liveness interaction as not covered), so the gate evidence cannot see the hole.
findings.fix: Add a loop rule: inside 'loop l', borrows may only name regions whose region block is inside l's body (forcing region-per-iteration), and bindings declared outside l may not be moved inside l (copies exempt).

findings.problem: Arena escape is unsound and unenforceable (critics C and D, both blocking). alloc_arena<'a,T>'s result mode/type is never stated; if it is 'own T' the value's type carries no trace of 'a, OWN-1 permits moving/returning it past region end, and STOR-4's claimed 'OWN-4 violation' cannot fire because OWN-4 governs only borrows — use-after-free in an accepted program (D1/SCOPE-3 violation), and the check is not locally decidable, contradicting META-2. alloc_heap's return type is equally unstated, so heap-ness is untracked for STOR-3 drop elaboration. No prototype STOR coverage. Mild fix conflict: C prefers a region-carrying owned type (own<'a> T / arena<'a,T>); D offers that or having alloc_arena return &mut 'a T (arena owns, borrow discipline contains it).
findings.fix: Surface the region in the type — pick ONE of: alloc_arena returns a region-carrying owned type own<'a> T whose moves/stores go through OWN-4's outlives machinery, or alloc_arena returns &mut 'a T. State alloc_heap's return type and how heap-ness is tracked for drop elaboration. Give one example.

findings.problem: The stack default with its 'unless the initializer is an explicit allocation call' clause is unanimously blocking (critics A, B, D). It (a) is a defaulting rule banned by the round-4 zero-context-dependence invariant and META-2; (b) is an exception clause banned by META-3; (c) directly contradicts STOR-1 ('every allocation names its storage class explicitly'); and (d) violates the round-2 MEMORY floor (every allocation carries a writer-stated contract) and the D2 corrected ruling (uniform verbose-everywhere, no elision).
findings.fix: Make storage explicit in one uniform spelling: either every let names its storage class in the mode position (let x: own stack i32 = ...) or every initializer is an explicit allocation call including alloc_stack<T>(v). Delete the default and the unless-clause.

findings.problem: The law-test harness as a discharge channel creates the banned third trust state (critics A and B, both blocking). A property-test harness is neither deterministic-checker-grade proof, a runtime check under trap=abort, nor a verified proof artifact — the only admissible discharge forms per round-3 decided law ('no WRITER-EMITTABLE third state; toolchain-gated ledger entries are the SOLE trusted-assertion class'). A false-but-test-passing associativity law consumed by a reassociation pass is a silent miscompile; it also contradicts SCOPE-2's own 'no third state'.
findings.fix: Restrict optimizer-usable law discharge to the stated-and-checked channel (static proof, runtime check under trap=abort, verified proof artifact) or gated-family ledger entries. The harness may remain as non-normative pre-gate evidence informing gate review, never as a trust source.

findings.problem: bool has two-to-three identities and spellings (critics B and D blocking; critic A major — severity conflict noted, resolved to blocking): a TYPE-1 primitive, a prelude enum per GRAM-5 ('enum Bool { True(); False(); }'), and literals true/false per FORM-5. FORM-3's case rule makes 'bool' as a type name ill-formed against 'Bool'; a writer cannot know whether match arms on a bool are legal, whether to emit true or True(), or what ieq returns. Violates FORM-1/META-1/META-4 and the round-2 one-spelling floor.
findings.fix: Pick one representation and state it once. B and D lean prelude-enum-only: delete bool from TYPE-1, delete true/false from FORM-5, dispatch via match True()/False(), and make ieq/ilt/ile/band/bor/bnot take and return Bool; A treats primitive-bool-with-a-dispatch-rule as an admissible alternative. Fix the bool/Bool case mismatch either way.

findings.problem: 'Accepted programs have no undefined behavior' states D1 unconditionally (critic A). The owner ruled D1a a Rust-class CONDITIONAL envelope, and round-3 recorded the honest Layer-4 statement as position-invariant law: safety holds conditional on ABI-well-behaved foreign code and the declared TCB. Even a pure-kernel program sits on the undelimited TCB (allocator, runtime, compiler/checker).
findings.fix: Restate SCOPE-3 conditionally: accepted programs have no UB conditional on the declared TCB (and, once gated/FFI constructs link, on ABI-well-behaved foreign code), cross-referencing the Layer-4 envelope statement.

findings.problem: The optimizer license for pure calls ('deduplicate, reorder, eliminate') is unsound without a termination requirement (critic D). pure excludes traps but not divergence (unbounded loop / recursion are expressible), and eliminating or reordering a diverging pure call changes observable behavior — the classic LLVM forward-progress hazard, contradicting SCOPE-3 and the round-3 checked-not-assumed spine.
findings.fix: Either restrict the license (dedup/reorder allowed; elimination of an unused pure call requires a termination proof) or add divergence to the effect row with pure implying termination via a conservative structural rule (every loop has a break on all paths; recursion needs a declared decreasing measure or is rejected in pure fns).

findings.problem: Rows 'checked both ways' plus proof-relative trap elimination makes acceptance prover-relative (critic D): whether a body 'exhibits' traps depends on what the checker can prove, so a prover upgrade flips the same program from required-to-declare to rejected-for-declaring — contradicting round-3 cross-cutting invariant 4 (acceptance is artifact-checkable and stable across toolchain versions). Cross-references the missing artifact-checkability rule (see the GLOBAL report-family/artifact finding).
findings.fix: Define 'exhibits' syntactically (any .trap op, check stmt, or bounds-checked index counts, even if later proven away) or make rows upper bounds with unexhibited effects surfaced as a lint — which also matches PROG-2's 'not exceeding' phrasing.

findings.problem: The prelude is normative but undefined (critics B and D blocking; critic A major via PROG-1 — resolved to blocking). GRAM-5, ERR-1, OP-2, PROG-1, STOR-2 all lean on prelude contents (Bool, Result<T,E>, Option<T>, Overflow, iadd family, cvt, index, alloc_*, band/bor/bnot), but no rule fixes variant names (Ok/Err? Some/None?) or any operation signature (modes, effect rows, generics). A writer cannot emit one exhaustive match over Result without guessing. This is a shadow spec under the round-4 no-shadow-spec decided law, and notes/calibration-audit-v0.json prices the 3208-token spec without it.
findings.fix: Add a counted, normative §Prelude annex with stable rule IDs: full canonical source of Bool/Result/Option and every op error type, plus a complete signature table (name, type params, operand modes/types, return type, effect row) for every kernel operation. Re-run the calibration audit.

findings.problem: Kernel-reserve pricing is incomplete against round-4 decided law — SEVERITY CONFLICT: critic A blocking, critic B major, critic D minor (resolved to major, middle). Round-4 requires the kernel (D1a calculus + obligation ledger + FFI frames + capability predicates) to be priced FIRST, but SCOPE-1/LAYER scope the ledger and FFI frames out and Send/Sync-class capability predicates (a round-2 'required core kernel component') appear nowhere, so the 3208-token audit priced the wrong artifact. FIX CONFLICT: A demands adding writer-visible stubs now plus an owner ruling per META-5 for any narrowing; B and D accept an explicitly recorded deferral (audit and decision-gates state the v0 baseline covers only the writer-facing subset; SCOPE-1 gains a one-sentence no-concurrency scope statement with capability predicates as a priced future delta).
findings.fix: Either add the writer-visible reserve items (capability-predicate rules; writer-visible ledger/FFI-frame schemas) to the counted kernel mass and re-run the audit, or record the narrowing explicitly in the audit and decision-gates with an owner ruling and declared spec delta. Do not leave the narrowing silent.

findings.problem: Two region spellings coexist — SEVERITY CONFLICT: critic D blocking, critic B major, critic A minor (resolved to major). OWN-3/OWN-4/STOR-1/STOR-2/EFF-1/PROG-2 use apostrophe forms ('a, 'static) that FORM-3's [a-z][a-z0-9_]* identifier rule makes ill-formed; OWN-2 and GRAM-4 use bare/undefined REGIONID; prototype tests already use bare 'a'. Two spellings for one construct violates FORM-1/META-1 and the canonical-form floor, and the writer will emit whichever spelling the rule it read used.
findings.fix: Define REGIONID's lexical form once in FORM-3 (pick one form) and rewrite every region occurrence in OWN/STOR/EFF/PROG, grammar productions, and examples to that single spelling. Run the META-4 duplicate-detection CI the spec promises.

findings.problem: Writer-facing artifact schemas are normatively referenced but absent and uncounted — SEVERITY CONFLICT: critic B blocking, critic D minor (resolved to major). The machine-readable trap report (SCOPE-3/OP-5/EFF-3), diagnostics vocabulary and node-path format (DIAG-1), site-enumerated edit list (ERR-2), and elaborated re-read artifact format (DIAG-2/STOR-3) are all inside the round-4 no-shadow-spec perimeter, and calibration-audit-v0.json confirms they are excluded from the 3208-token baseline. FIX CONFLICT: B requires them inside the counted document; D accepts explicit cross-references recording them as tracked, counted pending deltas.
findings.fix: Add compact field-table schemas for each (appendices are fine but inside the counted artifact), or at minimum convert each reference into an explicit tracked spec-delta obligation; then re-run the audit and restate the baseline in decision-gates.md.

findings.problem: The grammar is not closed (critics A, B, D major; critic C minor — noted): roughly 15 referenced nonterminals are never defined (type, mode, generics, targs, effects, region_params, path, literal, fn_sig, law, param/arg/type/binder lists, expr_stmt) nor are tokens STRING, LABEL, REGIONID; there is no spelling for passing region or const-generic arguments at call sites even though FN-2/round-2 law forbids inference. GRAM-1's LL(1)/isomorphism claims and SCOPE-2's 'parses under the canonical grammar' acceptance gate are therefore undefined, and under round-4's no-shadow-spec rule the missing productions are uncounted spec mass distorting the calibration baseline.
findings.fix: Add every missing production and token definition (including mode := own | & REGIONID | &mut REGIONID; a full type production; call-site region/const argument syntax; list separator rules), then re-price the spec and update the audit.

findings.problem: The LL(1)/unambiguity claims are false as written (critics B and D): place is left-recursive (never LL(1)); expr's place and call alternatives share FIRST token IDENT; index is derivable as both a place and a call (critic B's OP-1 finding: one construct, two syntactic homes, breaking the 1:1 production/node isomorphism); dotted operation names (iadd.wrap) have no lexical home since FORM-3 identifiers cannot contain '.' and '.' already means field projection; and there is no reserved-word list, so a binding named 'index' or 'match' is legal and unparseable.
findings.fix: Refactor to genuine LL(1) (place := IDENT suffix*), give index exactly ONE home (place-only or call-only, cross-referenced from the other section), define OPNAME as a single token class (or respell as iadd_wrap), enumerate reserved words, and add a spec-CI check that mechanically verifies the LL(1)/unambiguity claims.

findings.problem: Borrows cannot be returned or stored per the grammar, yet OWN-4 legislates both (critic C): fn_decl's return is '-> type' with no mode slot, fields are 'IDENT : type', and TYPE-2 has no borrow/reference type former — &r T exists only as a binding/param mode. So OWN-4's 'stored into a value or returned' is grammatically inexpressible (while a prototype positive test returns one), and slice<T> ('always borrowed') can never be a field or return type. Spec-internal contradiction guaranteeing writer/checker divergence.
findings.fix: Decide and state once: either add a borrow type former (&'r T usable in return position and struct fields with declared regions — what OWN-4 and the prototype assume), or forbid borrow returns/fields outright and delete OWN-4's return/store clause.

findings.problem: No expression form constructs any composite value — no struct literal, no enum-variant construction, no array construction (critic D blocking; critics A and B major — resolved to major, noting D's escalation). Whether variant construction counts as a 'call' is unstated (path undefined; FORM-3 reserves uppercase for types). As written the writer cannot create a Result, Option, Bool, env-struct (FN-5), or array, making ERR-1's errors-as-values and GRAM-5's Bool dispatch unimplementable.
findings.fix: Add explicit productions with one worked example each: variant construction (e.g. Ok<i32, Overflow>(x)), struct construction, array construction; define path to include TYPEID segments or give construction dedicated productions; state how targs interact with prelude generics.

findings.problem: Conformance is undeclarable (critics A and B major; critic D blocking — resolved to major, noting D's escalation): FN-3 requires explicit per-(type, contract) conformance but item := fn_decl | struct_decl | enum_decl | contract_decl has no conformance production, fn_sig and law are undefined nonterminals, and the bound spelling fn sum<T: Monoid> is unparseable because generics has no production. The decided contract-group kernel (required under every open outcome) can never be exercised: no type can satisfy T: Monoid, so no constrained generic can be instantiated.
findings.fix: Add a conform_decl item production (naming type, contract, and member bindings checked against the contract's fn_sigs), plus fn_sig, law, and generics-with-bounds productions, and the at-most-one-conformance coherence rule with its own rule ID and a worked example.

findings.problem: Match ownership semantics are entirely unspecified (critics B, C, D major; critic A minor — resolved to major): whether the scrutinee is moved or borrowed, what modes arm binders receive (binders carry no written mode, directly contradicting OWN-2's 'modes are always written; there is no default'), what binding by value through a shared borrow means (a move out of a borrowed place), and the binding-state join across arms (moved-in-one-arm: live or dead after?). Since match is the ONLY conditional/dispatch construct, every conditional in the language hits this hole; the checker prototype has necessarily decided it one way already.
findings.fix: Add uniform rules: matching an own scrutinee moves it and binds payload by move (or require written binder modes); matching through &r / &mut r yields binders of that borrow mode and region; conservative join — a binding moved in ANY arm is dead after the match. One worked example per case.

findings.problem: match_expr cannot produce a value (critic D): expr includes match_expr but arm bodies are '{ stmt* }' with no result expression or '=> expr' form. With no 'if' and let requiring an initializer, conditional initialization (let y = if c then a else b) — the most common pattern an AI writer needs — is inexpressible in both expression and statement form.
findings.fix: Either add a value-arm form (=> expr) for expression position — accepting the two-arm-shapes cost against the one-spelling rule, which D itself flags — or make match statement-only and add the canonical conditional-initialization idiom as a worked example (which requires deciding how an arm initializes a pre-bound binding).

findings.problem: The operation inventory is open-ended and exception-laden (critics B and D): 'likewise isub, imul, idiv' plus the parenthetical idiv carve-out is an inline exception list (META-3); ineg's mode set is ambiguous (i32::MIN negation overflows, so it matters); idiv's INT_MIN/-1 case is unmentioned; OP-3's 'fadd.strict<T> etc.' leaves the float set unenumerated with no float comparisons and no NaN policy (the classic silent-wrong trap: is feq total? NaN != NaN?); and TYPE-4's cvt matrix — which pairs exist, which are total vs Result-returning, what the error type is — is a dangling cross-reference to a §7 that contains no cvt. Round-2 decided law mandates typed operation tables; none exists.
findings.fix: Replace OP-2/OP-3 prose with ONE canonical operation table (operation, type domain, available modes, signature, effect row) where mode availability is table data, not exceptions; include cvt rows with the total-vs-Result classification and error type per pair, ineg's modes, idiv's INT_MIN/-1 behavior, and feq/flt/fle with an explicit IEEE-754 NaN rule.

findings.problem: The effect-row surface is undefined and semantically incomplete (critics B and D): no effects production, separator, or canonical ordering (two orderings = two spellings, violating FORM-1); allocates(stack) is incoherent (every function uses the stack — either an unstated exemption, META-3, or every fn must declare it, contradicting PROG-2's example); call-site row composition through region-argument substitution is unstated; and PROG-2's 'not exceeding' presumes a row-subsumption relation EFF-1's both-ways exactness never defines. Round-3 also flags the effect-row floor as having ZERO exemplar cards, so nothing can lean on precedent (critic A separately notes this gate is unrecorded — see minor finding).
findings.fix: Add effects := effect (',' effect)* with a fixed canonical order; define pure as the empty row's unique spelling; state 'stack frames are not allocations for EFF purposes' as a positive definition; define call-site row substitution; define row subsumption once (or restate PROG-2 as exact-row).

findings.problem: writes('static) is dangling (critics A, B, D unanimous): no rule defines a 'static region (OWN-3 admits only lexical and caller-supplied regions), the grammar has no static/global item for the effect to range over, FORM-3's identifier rule excludes the tick spelling, and 'not exceeding' presumes the undefined subsumption relation. A writer cannot produce a conforming main and the checker has nothing to check it against.
findings.fix: Either define the static region and a global/static item with storage and initialization semantics, or reduce main's permitted row to 'allocates(heap), traps' and delete the reference. Define or drop 'not exceeding' per the EFF-1 fix.

findings.problem: The outlives definition is self-contradicting and partial (critic B major; critics A/D touch it as minor — resolved to major): 'outlives iff strictly encloses' is immediately falsified by the caller-supplied-region clause (an exception-shaped addendum to an 'iff'), the strict form means 'a never outlives 'a (forcing OWN-4's ad-hoc 'outlives-or-equals'), and the relation between two DISTINCT caller-supplied regions is undefined although OWN-4 needs it. The prototype invents declaration-order semantics (checker.py:65-71: earlier-declared caller regions outlive later ones) that appear in no rule.
findings.fix: Replace with one total, reflexive relation: outlives-or-equals = same region, or strict block enclosure, or (caller-supplied, local); distinct caller-supplied regions are incomparable and any check requiring an order between them fails closed (per OWN-8). Fix the checker to match.

findings.problem: Borrow liveness endpoint is ambiguous and the resulting semantics is usability-hostile (critics C and D): OWN-6 says 'to the end of the ENCLOSING region block' while OWN-4 says the borrow's NAMED region bounds its life — different answers for a caller-region borrow written in an inner block; the prototype implements the named-region reading (checker.py:124-125). Under lexical liveness with no reborrow rule, f(&mut 'a x); g(&mut 'a x); in one region is illegal — every mutating call needs its own region block, a guaranteed high-frequency AI-writer rejection the spec never teaches (the D2 spec-as-teacher failure mode; rejection rates flagged unmeasured in checker-prototype-report.json). Call-argument temporary borrows bound to no binding are addressed by no rule.
findings.fix: Rewrite OWN-6 to the named-region reading (delete 'enclosing'), then either add a call-scoped temporary rule (an argument borrow not bound by let dies at the end of the call expression) or normatively document the region-per-mutating-call idiom with accepted/rejected examples in the worked-example pack.

findings.problem: The OWN rules say nothing about calls passing borrows (critic C): no region-argument substitution rule at call sites (and no obligation that passed regions be live), no statement that argument borrows are live accesses of their resolved places across the call, no rule that a callee's instantiated effect row counts as caller-side reads/writes under OWN-5 (so calling a writes('a) fn while an &'a borrow is live is unrejectable, breaking OWN-9), no disjointness requirement for two &mut arguments, and no region instantiation for returned borrows. FN-1 says callers rely only on signatures, but no rule consumes the signature at the call node; the prototype's call case just recurses into args (checker.py:185-188).
findings.fix: Add an OWN-CALL rule cluster: region-argument substitution; argument borrows treated as live accesses for the call; instantiated callee effect rows checked against live borrows under OWN-5; two &mut arguments must resolve to disjoint places; returned-borrow region = instantiated actual, then OWN-4 applies.

findings.problem: Partial moves conflate place and binding (critic C): moves target places (move x.f) but OWN-1 kills 'the source BINDING'. Whether moving x.f kills all of x (the prototype's whole-binding behavior, checker.py:156) or only x.f (requiring per-place deadness and partial-drop elaboration) is unstated; each choice changes accepted programs and drop elaboration.
findings.fix: State the v0 rule explicitly: moving any place rooted at binding b kills b entirely; reads of disjoint sibling fields after a partial move are OWN-1 errors requiring restructuring (consistent with OWN-8's posture).

findings.problem: slice<T> is a borrow with no region and no birth (critics C and D; B partially): no region parameter (so OWN-3/4/6 cannot bound its life and a stored view is exactly the escape case OWN-4 polices, with nothing to check), no creation operation anywhere (only index consumes one — slices are unconstructible), no len operation (loops over arrays cannot terminate on a queried bound), no OWN-7 overlap story (two slices over one array never overlap syntactically — if two &mut-mode slices are constructible, the OWN-9 noalias export is silently false, the known miscompile class), the index operand's integer type is unstated (no usize exists), and 'always borrowed, never owned' is a type-specific exception to OWN-2 (META-3).
findings.fix: Make it slice<'a, T> with an explicit region; add exactly one creation operation specified as a borrow of the underlying array place (plus len) so OWN-5/OWN-7 apply; state that slices over the same resolved root overlap conservatively; fix the index operand type (e.g. u64) in one rule.

findings.problem: array<T, N> introduces const generics with no constant-expression rules (critics A and D). The round-2 floor is a closed, decidable, trivially-terminating constant-expression sublanguage; with N's language unspecified, writer and checker will diverge on which lengths are writable and when two array types are equal, and const-generic argument spelling at instantiation sites is undefined.
findings.fix: Add CONST rules defining the closed sublanguage, or restrict N to literal integer constants in v0 and say so explicitly (including whether const args carry FORM-5 suffixes in targs).

findings.problem: Two SCOPE-2 defects (critics A and B): (a) 'There is no third state: nothing writer-stated is trusted unchecked' restates the PRE-amendment spine — the legislated form is 'no WRITER-EMITTABLE third state' with toolchain-gated ledger entries as the sole trusted-assertion class, and a kernel program relying on a gated law lives in a state SCOPE-2 says does not exist; (b) 'every checkable-but-unproven D1-critical fact carries a runtime check' uses the classification 'D1-critical' which the artifact never defines — the class assignment (bounds and checkable alias-disjointness in; overflow out, as a policy class) lives only in round-3 notes.
findings.fix: Reword to 'no writer-emittable third state', naming gated ledger entries as the sole trusted-assertion class (with the strict reading governing until the amendment formally issues), and add one rule enumerating the in-kernel D1-critical fact classes, cross-referenced from SCOPE-2 and OP-4.

findings.problem: 'The compiler may eliminate a check only by proof' leaves 'proof' undefined (critic A). Round-3 legislated proof = deterministic-checker-grade or verified-proof-artifact discharge, and fenced the solver: it may only PROMOTE performance facts, never gate acceptance or elide safety-relevant checks until a proof-checker TCB exists. As written, raw SMT output could count as proof.
findings.fix: Define 'proof' per the round-3 legislated definition in OP-4 (or one shared definition rule) and state the solver fence: solver-promoted facts are performance-ledger only and never license check elision.

findings.problem: The decided error-handling replacement is only half-present (critic A): missing are verifier-checked Result propagation with auto-derived context/cause chains at zero hand-written tokens per site — explicitly the argument that neutralized unwinding — plus the language-enforced Result-vs-trap classification rule and anti-sentinel lints. Bare manual match propagation reopens the objection the round-2 verdict priced away.
findings.fix: Add the propagation operation with auto-derived context chains and the Result-vs-trap classification rule, or record an explicit deferral with its spec delta per META-5.

findings.problem: The closures/dispatch replacement floors are named but not specified (critic A): no guaranteed direct-call monomorphization for env-struct dispatch (never fn-pointer+env indirection), none of the mandated exactness diagnostics (unused env field, used-but-uninitialized field, dead dispatch variant, non-exhaustive tag dispatch, signature mismatch — each naming struct/function/construction site), and typed operation tables with construction-checked payload/table coupling and the one-canonical-table-per-(variant-set x operation) rule are absent entirely.
findings.fix: Add rules for the direct-call guarantee and the mandated diagnostic set; add the typed-operation-table construct with its one-definition and construction-coupling rules, or record explicit deferrals with spec deltas.

findings.problem: The storage-contract vocabulary omits the decided foreign-shared allocation-site field (critic A): round-3 decided foreign-retainable memory is pre-declared foreign-shared at its allocation site as one field on the existing storage-contract floor, and REJECTED compiler-inferred call-site demotion. The spec is silent, so the storage grammar must break when FFI frames land, and the demotion rejection is recorded nowhere normative.
findings.fix: Reserve the foreign-shared field in STOR-1's vocabulary now, legal only in programs containing gated FFI frames, and record that call-site demotion is a floor violation.

findings.problem: The decided report family and artifact-checkability are missing (critic A, two findings merged; corroborated by critic D's EFF-1 prover-relativity finding): per-function check reports, per-function lifetime reports, and check-density/elision reports are decided-law toolchain outputs attached to the canonical artifact, and round-3 invariant (4) requires proof objects and check instrumentation to travel with the program so 'accepted' is artifact-checkable, not process-relative. The spec defines acceptance purely as a process outcome and DIAG-2 omits the report family.
findings.fix: Add DIAG rules: (a) the normative report family enumerated as toolchain output accompanying the canonical artifact; (b) the canonical elaborated artifact embeds proof objects and check instrumentation, and acceptance is decidable from the artifact alone across toolchain versions.

findings.problem: The contract-edit gating floor is unstated (critic A): round-2 decided that editing a declared contract (signatures, law bundles, lifetime/storage contracts) is a privileged, gated toolchain operation so a writer cannot launder a hallucinated body by rewriting its declaration, and round-3 unified this into one gating regime. This is writer-facing law — it bounds what the writer may edit — and the kernel spec omits it entirely.
findings.fix: Add a rule: contract, signature, law, and storage-contract edits are one privileged gated toolchain operation with one audit trail, outside steady-state writer capability.

findings.problem: Name-binding rules are missing entirely (critic D major; critic B minor via DIAG-1 — resolved to major): nothing legislates declaration-before-use, shadowing/redeclaration legality, label/region namespaces, or break-label scoping. The prototype exposes the gap by misappropriating TYPE-5 (a type-inference rule) for 'unknown binding' (checker.py:198) because no correct rule ID exists — already breaking DIAG-1's rule-to-emitter bijection at prototype scale.
findings.fix: Add rules: declaration-before-use; no shadowing/redeclaration of a live name (one uniform rule); LABELs and REGIONIDs get their own namespaces under the same rule; break's LABEL must name a lexically enclosing loop. Retarget the checker's diagnostic and add the rule-to-emitter coverage audit to spec CI now, while there is one emitter.

findings.problem: Return totality and dead code are unspecified (critic D): must every path end in return/trap, does a unit function return implicitly, and is code after return/break rejected? Each choice changes what the writer must emit for main() -> unit, and the checker will pick one silently.
findings.fix: Add: every function body returns on all paths ('return unit;' mandatory — no implicit return, matching the no-elision doctrine); statements after return/break in the same block are a hard error citing this rule.

findings.problem: FORM-1's 'exactly one legal byte-level formatting' is not actually pinned down (critics B and D): required-ness of token separation is undefined, comma conventions, line-break/wrapping rules (GRAM-3's own fn_decl display wraps under no stated rule), nesting-level definition for match arms, blank lines, and EOF newline are all unstated. Two conforming implementations will disagree on canonical bytes, and FORM-1 makes one of them a hard error chosen by private convention.
findings.fix: Complete FORM-2: token-separation table, comma rule (one space after, none before), a deterministic wrap rule or explicit no-wrap, one nesting level per '{' block including match arms, blank-line rules, exactly one trailing LF. Add byte-level golden tests to spec CI and exhibit it in the worked example.

findings.problem: STRING is used by the grammar (doc STRING, check ... trap STRING) but FORM-5's exhaustive literal list excludes strings, and STRING's lexical form — delimiters, escape set, canonical escape policy — is defined nowhere (critics B and D). Both an internal contradiction and an undefined byte-canonical surface (unnormalized escapes = multiple spellings per value).
findings.fix: Add STRING to FORM-5 with a complete lexical definition including a canonical escape policy (one spelling per string value), or replace strings in doc/trap with a defined restricted form.

findings.problem: Zero worked examples exist in the artifact (critic D). FORM-1/FORM-2 legislate a single legal byte formatting, but no complete program is shown, so canonical bytes are unlearnable for a sole in-context teaching artifact. Round-4 makes the teaching pack (spec + canonical worked examples) the delivery unit, and calibration-audit-v0.json itself flags examples as pending (+2-4k tokens). Highest-leverage single addition per critic D.
findings.fix: Add a §Examples section: at minimum one complete byte-exact accepted program (main + generic fn with contract bound + region + borrow + match on Result + check + checked arithmetic) plus per-construct micro-examples (env-struct, arena allocation, enum construction). Mark examples normative for FORM rules and count them in the audit.

findings.problem: 'Strict IEEE 754 by default' is incoherent under one-mode-per-node (there are no defaults if every node names .strict), and remanding fast-math/approximation permissions to a LAYER decides a placement that numeric-semantics still holds open (critic A; round-3 decided only overflow, not float-relaxation placement).
findings.fix: Reword: 'v0 defines only .strict float modes; each node names its mode.' Record approximation/fast-math modes as an open numeric-semantics question rather than a decided LAYER.

findings.problem: 'Every rule is locally decidable at its node given declared types in scope' overstates the round-4 invariant (a regularity constraint on spellings/rules, not node-local decidability) and is falsified by the spec's own flow-dependent checks: OWN-5 exclusivity and OWN-6 liveness need knowledge of other live borrows (critic A; critic C's STOR-4 finding independently shows a rule that is not locally decidable as written).
findings.fix: Reword META-2 to the round-4 formulation (no context-dependent spellings or rule variants) and drop the node-local decidability claim.

findings.problem: META-3 ('no rule carries an exception clause') is violated by the draft it governs: OWN-5 ('except through that borrow'), OWN-7 ('unless indices are distinct constants'), STOR-2 ('unless the initializer is...'), plus OP-2's idiv parenthetical and TYPE-2's slice carve-out (critics B, C, D collectively). If META-3 is CI-checked as claimed, spec CI fails on day one; if these are not 'exceptions', the term is undefined. Largely resolved as a side effect of the blocking OWN-5/STOR-2 fixes.
findings.fix: Restate the remaining rules in positive/total form (e.g. OWN-7: 'index places with the same base overlap iff their indices are not both constants with unequal values') or define 'exception clause' precisely in META-3.

findings.problem: Stated-once violations the spec's own CI rule would fail (critic B): trap=abort stated three times, proven-else-checked three times, match-as-only-dispatch four times, gated-family-not-writer-writable twice, check's spelling twice, and OWN-9 restates OWN-1/OWN-5 consequences as fresh normative text.
findings.fix: Keep one normative statement per fact and convert other mentions to bare rule-ID cross-references; recast OWN-9 as an explicitly derived non-normative consequences note or delete it.

findings.problem: 'Owned values are unaliased' is false while shared borrows of them are live (owner remains readable under OWN-5, so owner path and borrow path alias for reads) — and once the OWN-5/OWN-7 holes are fixed, this optimizer-facing sentence should be re-derived, not asserted (critic C).
findings.fix: Qualify ('owned values are unaliased except by live shared borrows, which are read-only for their duration') or drop the bullet, keeping only the &mut-noalias and shared-read-only bullets.

findings.problem: Reinitialization after move is unspecified (critic C): whether 'set x = e' on a moved x is a use of a dead binding (the prototype rejects, checker.py:212) or a revival is unstated; Rust-prior writers will expect revival and the checker will reject.
findings.fix: State it: a moved binding is dead for all statements including set; reinitialization requires a new let.

findings.problem: Region name uniqueness/shadowing is unspecified (critic C): nothing forbids nested regions with the same name or a local region shadowing a caller region param, and the prototype's index-based region depth silently miscomputes outlives under shadowing (checker.py:66-67).
findings.fix: Add to OWN-3: region identifiers are unique within a function (params included); duplicate introduction is an error.

findings.problem: Non-lexical region exits are unhandled by the elaboration story (critic C): break LABEL and return can cross region boundaries, but STOR-3/STOR-4 derive drops and arena releases from 'region ends' only, and DIAG-2 requires every derived drop explicit in the artifact — where releases attach on early-exit paths is unstated.
findings.fix: State that every control-flow edge leaving a region block (fallthrough, break, return) carries the region's releases/drops in the elaborated artifact, in one stated order (e.g. reverse declaration order).

findings.problem: law has no production and no vocabulary (critic D): contract_decl shows law* but law := ... does not exist, and whether law names (associative, commutative, ...) form a closed harness-understood set or are free-form, with what arities, is unstated. A writer cannot emit a law without guessing. (Separate from the blocking FN-4 trust-channel finding.)
findings.fix: Add law := 'law' LAWNAME '(' IDENT (',' IDENT)* ')' ';' with a closed LAWNAME table (name, arity, checked property), stated once.

findings.problem: 'Polymorphic recursion is not permitted (instantiation closure must be finite)' names a property, not a decidable rule (critic D): the writer cannot predict which recursive generic patterns are rejected and no rule ID exists for the diagnostic to cite the procedure.
findings.fix: State the conservative decidable rule (e.g. in any call cycle, every generic function is called at exactly its own type parameters — a syntactic check) so rejection is predictable and locally explainable.

findings.problem: The effect-row vocabulary is instantiated while its named blocking evidence is undischarged (critic A): region/effect verifier exemplar cards (Cyclone, MLKit, Koka, DPJ) are blocking for the effect-row floor and currently number zero; the draft does not record this gate on section 9.
findings.fix: Mark section 9 in the status header as gated on region/effect exemplar carding before ratification.

findings.problem: The optional stated-and-checked channel (loop invariants, ranges, loop-bounded disjointness — fact-creating but always checked) has no kernel surface, and the spec does not say whether a passed check is fact-creating (its fact joining the proven set on the dominated path, the guard-then-assume pattern) or purely a trap assertion (critic A).
findings.fix: State check's fact-creating status in OP-5 and record the fuller stated-and-checked vocabulary as an explicitly deferred channel with its spec delta.

findings.problem: Section 5's provisional status is unrecorded (critics A and C converge): the owner attached a ratification precondition — formalized-calculus adoption (Featherweight Rust/Oxide/Austral-class) before ratification — and critic C notes the two inverted/exception-clause OWN bugs found in this review are exactly the class a formal-core comparison catches. A reader of the spec alone cannot see OWN-1..9 are provisional.
findings.fix: Add to the status header (or section 5 preamble) that OWN rules must be reconciled against a formalized minimal calculus before ratification, citing the D1a gate.

findings.problem: Wrong cross-reference: 'Documentation is the doc field of declarations (§4)' — §4 is Types; the doc production lives in §3/GRAM-3 (critic B).
findings.fix: Change the citation to §3/GRAM-3 and prefer rule-ID citations over section numbers throughout.

findings.problem: The token 'unit' is simultaneously a type (TYPE-1) and a value literal (FORM-5) — one spelling, two constructs, resolved by position, brushing against the zero-context-dependence invariant's 'no positionally-resolved meaning' (critic B).
findings.fix: Split the spellings or add an explicit note-rule that type and literal namespaces are disjoint grammar positions, making the resolution production-local rather than contextual.

findings.problem: The parenthetical '(Flow-sensitive shortening is a permitted future refinement...)' embeds a future-amendment permission inside a normative rule — spec mass that teaches the writer nothing and belongs in the decision gates, where it already exists under the D1a checker levers (critic B).
findings.fix: Delete the parenthetical; the NLL-refinement option stays recorded in decision-gates.md.

findings.problem: The audited baseline '59 rules' counts rule-ID occurrences including two cross-reference citations; the artifact defines 57 unique rules (critic B). The spec-delta ratchet starts from a miscounted baseline and cross-references will inflate future counts — exactly the individuation gaming the round-4 open tension warns about.
findings.fix: Recount using definition-position IDs only (57), update decision-gates.md and calibration-audit-v0.json, and fold 'citations do not count as rules' into the canonical rule-individuation procedure.

missingRules: PRELUDE annex (all four critics): counted, normative canonical source of Bool, Result<T,E>, Option<T>, and every operation error type (Overflow, division, cvt narrowing) with exact variant names; a complete signature table for every kernel operation (type params, operand modes/types, return type, effect row); and a rule that prelude names are in scope in every program and may not be redefined.

GRAM completion (all four critics): productions for type, mode, generics (type/const/region params with bounds), targs (type/region/const arguments at call sites — inference is forbidden, so call-site region/const argument syntax is mandatory), effects, region_params, path or a single-token OPNAME class, literal, expr_stmt, fn_sig, law, and all list forms with separators; token definitions for STRING (closed escape set), LABEL, REGIONID; a reserved-word list excluded from IDENT; a conformance-declaration item production; composite construction forms (struct, enum-variant, array); and a borrow type former for return/field positions (or an explicit prohibition, deleting OWN-4's store/return clause).

Copy class and move spelling (critics B, C, D): which types copy on use (TYPE-1 primitives, shared borrows) vs are affine (own composites, &mut borrows), plus an explicit 'move p' expression in GRAM-4 — currently only a comment and node-kind split in prototype/checker/checker.py:19-21.

Place resolution / holder / dereference (critics A, B, C): normative definition of 'holder'; places rooted at ref-mode bindings resolve to the borrowed place plus appended path; OWN-5/OWN-7 judged on resolved places; an explicit deref spelling (per A/B) or deref-transparent rule (per C — approach conflict to resolve); no borrowing a binding that holds a live &mut; and no moving content reached through any borrow.

OWN-10 borrow-storage outlives (critic C): a borrow &'a p is legal only if p's storage outlives 'a — own bindings require 'a introduced within the binding's scope; through-borrow places require the source borrow's region to outlive-or-equal 'a. Closes the dangling-return hole enshrined by test_return_caller_region_borrow.
Loop back-edge rule (critic C): inside a loop, borrows may only name regions introduced inside the loop body, and bindings declared outside the loop may not be moved inside it (copies exempt).

Match ownership rules (critics B, C, D; critic A rated minor): scrutinee move-vs-borrow semantics, arm-binder modes (written or uniformly derived — OWN-2 currently forbids the unstated default), and the conservative join (a binding moved in any arm is dead after the match).

OWN-CALL boundary cluster (critic C): region-argument substitution at call nodes; argument borrows as live accesses of resolved places across the call; callee effect rows instantiated at actuals and checked against live borrows under OWN-5; disjointness of resolved places for two &mut arguments; returned-borrow region instantiation feeding OWN-4.
Partial-move rule (critic C): moving any place rooted at binding b kills b entirely in v0 (matches the prototype's whole-binding kill); stated rather than left ambiguous in OWN-1.
No-reinitialization rule (critic C): set on a moved binding is a use of a dead binding; revival requires a new let.

Total outlives relation and caller-region ordering (critics B, C, D): one reflexive outlives-or-equals definition; distinct caller-supplied regions are incomparable and order-requiring checks fail closed (the prototype currently invents declaration-order outlives, checker.py:65-71); region-name uniqueness/no-shadowing within a function.

Region-carrying allocation result types (critics C, D): the mode/type returned by alloc_arena<'a,T> (own<'a> T or &mut 'a T — pick one) and alloc_heap<T>, so arena escape is locally decidable (META-2) and enforced by OWN-4 machinery; heap-ness tracking for drop elaboration.
Region-exit elaboration (critic C): every edge leaving a region block (fallthrough, break, return) carries that region's drops/arena releases in the DIAG-2 artifact, in one stated order.

STOR foreign-shared field (critic A): the foreign-shared allocation-site field on the storage contract, legal only with gated FFI frames, plus the recorded rejection of compiler-inferred call-site demotion.

Slice and index rules (critics C, D): slice<'a, T> with a region; exactly one slice-creation operation specified as a borrow of the underlying array place; len; conservative overlap for slices over the same resolved root; the integer type of array/slice indices and of const generic N (no usize exists in TYPE-1).
CONST-1 (critics A, D): the closed, decidable, trivially-terminating constant-expression sublanguage governing const generics and array lengths — or an explicit literals-only v0 restriction.

Capability predicates and concurrency scope (critics A, B, D — severity/fix conflict noted in findings): CAP rules for Send/Sync-class type-level capability predicates (data races prevented; general race conditions out of scope per amended C004), or at minimum an explicit SCOPE-1 statement that kernel v0 is single-threaded with capability predicates as a priced future spec delta per the round-4 kernel-reserve sequencing.

GATE-1 contract-edit gating (critic A): contract, signature, law-bundle, and storage-contract edits are one privileged gated toolchain operation with one audit trail, outside steady-state writer capability (round-2 AI-writer additions; round-3 gating unification).

LEDGER stub (critic A): a writer-visible normative stub for the gated fact-boundary family — one family, one per-fact obligation ledger, manifest-free members unrepresentable, writer cannot author or edit — so kernel programs' relationship to gated law (e.g. FN-4 discharge) is defined and the round-4 pricing unit is complete.

DIAG report family (critic A): machine-readable per-function check reports, per-function lifetime reports, and check-density/elision reports as normative toolchain output accompanying the canonical artifact.

DIAG artifact-checkability (critics A, D): proof objects and check instrumentation travel with the canonical elaborated artifact; acceptance is decidable from the artifact alone across toolchain versions (round-3 invariant 4).

Writer-facing schemas per the no-shadow-spec perimeter (critics A, B, D — in-artifact vs tracked-deferral conflict noted in findings): trap-report schema (machine-readable, descriptive CFI/frame-pointer stack attribution, no executable unwind semantics), diagnostics vocabulary and node-path format, site-enumerated edit-list schema, and the elaborated re-read artifact format.

ERR-3 (critic A): language-enforced Result-vs-trap classification; verifier-checked Result propagation with auto-derived context/cause chains at zero hand-written tokens per site; anti-sentinel lint contract.

DISP-1 typed operation tables (critic A): named fields, per-row signature checks, construction-checked payload/table coupling, one canonical table per (variant-set x operation), ad-hoc switch sites lowered or flagged.

FN-7 env-struct floors (critic A): guaranteed direct-call monomorphization (never fn-pointer+env indirection) and the mandated exactness diagnostics (unused env field, used-but-uninitialized field, dead dispatch variant, non-exhaustive tag dispatch, signature mismatch — each naming struct/function/construction site).

Name-resolution/scoping rules (critics B, D): declaration-before-use, no shadowing/redeclaration, separate namespaces for LABELs and REGIONIDs, break-label must name an enclosing loop — with their own rule IDs so the checker stops misappropriating TYPE-5.
Return-totality and dead-code rules (critic D): all paths return ('return unit;' mandatory); statements after return/break are hard errors.

Effect-row completion (critics B, D): row grammar with fixed canonical ordering; pure as the empty row's unique spelling; the definition of allocates (stack frames excluded by definition, not exception); call-site row substitution through region arguments; the row-subsumption relation PROG-2 presumes, or its removal.
'static and global state (critics A, B, D): definition of the 'static region and a static/global item with storage and initialization semantics, or deletion of writes('static) from PROG-2.

D1-critical fact-class enumeration (critic B): which checkable fact classes obligate proven-else-checked in-kernel (bounds; alias-disjointness where a check form exists) and the explicit statement that overflow is a policy class outside it.

Termination/divergence policy for pure functions (critic D): either a divergence effect / conservative termination rule, or a restricted EFF-2 elimination license requiring termination proof — currently an unsoundness.

FORM-2 completion (critics B, D): token-separation table, comma rules, deterministic wrap or no-wrap rule, nesting-level definition for match arms, blank-line rules, EOF newline — with byte-level golden tests in spec CI.

Worked-examples section (critic D; flagged pending in calibration-audit-v0.json): at least one complete byte-exact accepted program plus per-construct micro-examples (env-struct, Result match, region+borrow, arena allocation, checked arithmetic), normative for FORM rules and counted in the audit.
Match-value form or canonical conditional-initialization idiom (critic D): either '=> expr' arms in expression position or a statement-only match with a documented canonical initialization idiom.
Entry-point rule (critic B): existence and uniqueness of main (PROG-2 constrains its signature but nothing requires exactly one).
Conservative-extension statement (critic B): unknown constructs at kernel extension points are deterministic hard errors (round-4 retained law; currently only implied by FORM-1).

Process gate, non-spec (critics A, C): reconcile the repaired OWN calculus against Featherweight Rust / Oxide / Austral formalizations before ratification and record the D1a gate in the status header — the inverted OWN-4 direction and the OWN-5 exception-clause bug found in this review are exactly the class a formal-core comparison catches.
